2026-02-28 16:13:13
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF project is “Ember & Ice”! 🎧 Listen: https://www.tryquinn.com/series/ember-and-ice IMO: As a huge fan of the romantasy genre, this was so fun! I love that Hudson & Connor were both willing to try something new. Hudson’s voice is really nice to listen to and I’d love to hear him try voice acting again in the future. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-28 13:31:29
.@ELLEINDIA: From Gen Z To Gen X: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Is Changing How India Watches Queer Stories “Hudson Williams poured his heart and soul into every scene, creating a performance that feels once-in-a-lifetime, truly generational. Both [Hudson and Connor] are stars of their era, rising at a pace rarely seen. (…) They’ve captured hearts across borders, with a fandom journey from Canada to India that’s already cementing them as some of the biggest names in the industry today.” “Shane, neurodivergent-coded, anxious, holding his clothes nervously before getting into bed, is not mocked for his sensitivity. Ilya, the supposed playboy, is allowed tenderness. Two hyper-masculine athletes playing a hyper-masculine sport reveal emotional vulnerability without surrendering their masculinity. That dismantling matters.” Read more here: https://elle.in/life-culture/from-gen-z-to-gen-x-heated-rivalry-is-changing-how-india-watches-queer-stories-11160874

original tweet

2026-02-27 17:11:01
Yerin Ha on meeting Hudson Williams at the Gold House Lunar New Year celebration recently (via @MaxJGao) “I was even a little bit nervous talking to him, but it was really nice just seeing how he's navigating it. (…) And immediately, we were sharing our experiences and being vulnerable, and that's great. (…) Just being able to actually share stories and stuff like that was really nice. I'm very grateful that I got to meet him.”

original tweet

2026-02-27 17:11:01
Yerin Ha on meeting Hudson Williams at the Gold House Lunar New Year celebration recently (via @MaxJGao) “I was even a little bit nervous talking to him, but it was really nice just seeing how he's navigating it. (…) And immediately, we were sharing our experiences and being vulnerable, and that's great. (…) Just being able to actually share stories and stuff like that was really nice. I'm very grateful that I got to meet him.”

2026-02-27 16:16:38
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short films are “The Ticket” and “And…Action!” Hudson also produced “The Ticket”! 🎥 Links to watch: https://youtu.be/xtsJE2sI_qk?si=ysNZGMuYEA4M2aKg & https://youtu.be/WJSpyfSZ3cY?si=5MsMc2bqYPwwPrzK IMO: I love how Hudson’s character’s annoyance builds through his expressions in “The Ticket,” before he finally has enough of the other character. I really enjoyed “And…Action!,” and the way it cuts between black and white and color. Both films are a testament to how great Hudson is in comedic roles and how well he communicates through his micro-expressions. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-27 14:58:07
“Heated Rivalry” editor Arthur Tarnowski on watching Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie evolve whilst filming the show “I saw them change in the role. I saw them change as actors. Because, I think for them, it was probably the biggest sort of piece of work that they ever had to do. So there was this evolution, which was great, because it was completely in parallel with the characters being comfortable, being sort of assert themselves. I think this show is a result of a lot of these kind of perfect storms.” 🔗 https://cceditors.ca/2026/02/episode-115-heated-rivalry-with-veronique-barbe-cce-arthur-tarnowski-ace-cce/

original tweet

So there was this evolution, which was great, because it was completely in parallel with the characters being comfortable, being sort of assert themselves. I think this show is a result of a lot of these kind of perfect storms.” 🔗 https://cceditors.ca/2026/02/episode-115-heated-rivalry-with-veronique-barbe-cce-arthur-tarnowski-ace-cce/

original tweet

2026-02-27 14:46:59
“Heated Rivalry” editor Arthur Tarnowski reveals that the opening scene where Shane Hollander introduces himself to Ilya Rozanov was almost cut entirely from the show, but editors who worked on the trailer convinced Jacob Tierney to keep it in Previously, Hudson Williams has said that the scene was originally 6 minutes long of Shane attempting to make small talk, before it being cut to the 30 seconds that we see in the final edit It was also a mini-Easter egg that Ilya couldn’t light his cigarette until after he sees Shane 🔗 https://cceditors.ca/2026/02/episode-115-heated-rivalry-with-veronique-barbe-cce-arthur-tarnowski-ace-cce/

original tweet

2026-02-26 17:36:54
“Shane is not a character who explains himself. He internalises. He listens. He absorbs. Which is precisely why casting him was such a challenge. It’s the contrast that makes the performance so striking. Off-screen, Hudson is buoyant and playful. On-screen, Shane is tightly wound and emotionally guarded. The transformation is not cosmetic — it’s behavioural. Jacob Tierney the director, expands on why Shane was particularly difficult to bring to life: What Hudson had right away when I saw his tape was that he had, which it turns out was a totally wrong read on my part about him — being serious. But he is a good actor. In his seriousness, that came through when he read, and he had an ability, Shane was actually a really tricky character to write and a really tricky character to execute because so much of him is internal, and that requires a lot of active listening and a lot of letting your face do the work because he doesn't talk a lot, and I wasn't going to make him talk a lot because that was not part of who he is. And so he had to express a lot of things physically again, and I think that Hudson actually is an incredibly physical actor, and it shines through for Shane and makes it that you understand what he's feeling every step of the way, and that is a minor miracle with this performance.” 🔗 https://elle.in/life-culture/elle-exclusive-hudson-williams-and-connor-storrie-on-bringing-heated-rivalry-to-life-11125166

original tweet

2026-02-26 17:36:54
“Shane is not a character who explains himself. He internalises. He listens. He absorbs. Which is precisely why casting him was such a challenge. It’s the contrast that makes the performance so striking. Off-screen, Hudson is buoyant and playful. On-screen, Shane is tightly wound and emotionally guarded. The transformation is not cosmetic — it’s behavioural. Jacob Tierney the director, expands on why Shane was particularly difficult to bring to life: What Hudson had right away when I saw his tape was that he had, which it turns out was a totally wrong read on my part about him — being serious. But he is a good actor. In his seriousness, that came through when he read, and he had an ability, Shane was actually a really tricky character to write and a really tricky character to execute because so much of him is internal, and that requires a lot of active listening and a lot of letting your face do the work because he doesn't talk a lot, and I wasn't going to make him talk a lot because that was not part of who he is. And so he had to express a lot of things physically again, and I think that Hudson actually is an incredibly physical actor, and it shines through for Shane and makes it that you understand what he's feeling every step of the way, and that is a minor miracle with this performance.” 🔗 https://elle.in/life-culture/elle-exclusive-hudson-williams-and-connor-storrie-on-bringing-heated-rivalry-to-life-11125166

original tweet

2026-02-26 17:36:54
“Shane is not a character who explains himself. He internalises. He listens. He absorbs. Which is precisely why casting him was such a challenge. It’s the contrast that makes the performance so striking. Off-screen, Hudson is buoyant and playful. On-screen, Shane is tightly wound and emotionally guarded. The transformation is not cosmetic — it’s behavioural. Jacob Tierney the director, expands on why Shane was particularly difficult to bring to life: What Hudson had right away when I saw his tape was that he had, which it turns out was a totally wrong read on my part about him — being serious. But he is a good actor. In his seriousness, that came through when he read, and he had an ability, Shane was actually a really tricky character to write and a really tricky character to execute because so much of him is internal, and that requires a lot of active listening and a lot of letting your face do the work because he doesn't talk a lot, and I wasn't going to make him talk a lot because that was not part of who he is. And so he had to express a lot of things physically again, and I think that Hudson actually is an incredibly physical actor, and it shines through for Shane and makes it that you understand what he's feeling every step of the way, and that is a minor miracle with this performance.” 🔗 https://elle.in/life-culture/elle-exclusive-hudson-williams-and-connor-storrie-on-bringing-heated-rivalry-to-life-11125166

LangaraCollege via Instagram. “Want to walk in @ hudsonwilliamsofficial's footsteps? Sign up now for LangaraFest, happening on Wed, Mar 4 from 4-7pm. Register to attend at the link in bio and be entered to win a $1,000 tuition prize. Explore campus, take part in demo classes, chat with instructors and students, and learn more about the programs that interest you. Bring your parents, friends, or just yourself. We look forward to meeting you! stills of Hudson Williams' student work courtesy of @langarafilmarts.”

2026-02-25 16:15:58
🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/JBd4lNgQSxg?si=GNvscK9FmYm9TwHr IMO: This music video has an old horror movie vibe that I really love. I’m really into the juxtaposition of the red, warm lighting for the band and the blue, cooler toned lighting for Hudson’s character. The makeup is incredible, adding to the unsettling atmosphere. Paired with the song, the whole video carries a perfectly ominous energy from start to finish. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-24 16:54:00
The cottage featured in episode 6 of “Heated Rivalry” will be available to book for stays on @Airbnb from March 3rd Initially, it will cost $248.10 CAD per night — a nod to Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov’s respective jersey numbers: 24 and 81. 🔗 More info: https://www.thestar.com/life/travel/the-famed-cottage-from-heated-rivalry-will-soon-be-bookable-on-airbnb/article_66a7826d-f573-5bda-8580-b1d75823723c.html

2026-02-24 15:23:12
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Hold Your Back”! In addition to acting in this, Hudson also produced it! Trigger Warning: Domestic violence 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/O1ebmWVH8g4?si=pGjtfdVZ5YLlIhS9 IMO: Hudson’s range is incredible. Him and Zack did an amazing job with this film. It’s so short, yet so captivating and devastating. When I first watched it, the muted lighting is one of the first things I noticed—it feels cold, like their love has been gone for long time. I love the parallels between the first and last scene and the shot from above of them lying on the floor together. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

.@IndieWire reports that “Heated Rivalry” is “by far the biggest Crave Original to date, and is potentially on the podium [to become] one of the biggest Canadian dramas ever” The series is now approaching 11.5 Million viewers in the US, gaining 1 Million viewers in less than 2 weeks @CraveCanada has also experienced a 26% uplift in subscribers since 2025, which it credits to the show 🔗 https://www.indiewire.com/news/business/what-is-crave-canadian-streamer-heated-rivalry-interview-1235180578/

Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Peaceful Protest Turned Heated Rivalry”! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/djJQ3tGhlsQ?si=Oz_1amKTx4Xe8Eaa IMO: This gave me a lot of nostalgia for the early YouTube days. I love the way the fight is filmed. The blocking and choreography are great and you can tell they put a lot of thought into how each hit would look on camera. I also love Hudson in the last scene. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-22

2026-02-22 15:26:01
IMO: This is the project that gave us Hudson Williams, so we’ll be forever grateful. I think we can all agree that Shane is such a unique, loving, intelligent, and caring character—my favorite of all time. My favorite Shane scene is his coming out to Rose. I can feel Shane’s pain in my chest. It’s such a beautiful portrayal. My favorite Hollanov scene is when Shane is grilling eight burgers and says, “Leave me alone,” because Rozanov is teasing him. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-21 15:28:37
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Peace of Mind”! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/eNRndkfMZ9I?si=l9EJJYyxd4ltISLJ IMO: I love this one. It builds tension so well and Hudson is always so captivating to watch. The plot twist at the end?! 😭 Those final scenes are so vulnerable and I really love the switch from color to black and white. One of my favourite quotes: “Peace of mind was never about achieving your dreams or fixing your past, it’s about forgiving the ones that never happen and letting go of what you can’t change.” Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-21 03:57:55
“Yaga’s imprint – still has copies in stock on their own website, but the company has put in an unprecedented order of 1,000 copies for the play’s next reprint, after previous Yaga reprints of no more than 200 copies at a time. Elsewhere, the book is currently sold out on Amazon and Indigo’s websites, as well as Barnes and Noble’s website in the U.S. […] We’re receiving Yaga orders from all over the world,” she said, adding that approximately 80 per cent of the surprise sales have been e-books. “Iceland, Hungary, Australia, Poland, Romania, Singapore. Hong Kong. Finland. It just goes on and on. […] This early on, before Yaga has even hit the airwaves, it’s tough to forecast how long sales will continue to rise.” 🔗 https://www.theglobeandmail.com/culture/books/article-kat-sandler-play-yaga-sells-out-after-heated-rivalry-star-cast-in/

original tweet

2026-02-21 03:57:55
“Yaga’s imprint – still has copies in stock on their own website, but the company has put in an unprecedented order of 1,000 copies for the play’s next reprint, after previous Yaga reprints of no more than 200 copies at a time. Elsewhere, the book is currently sold out on Amazon and Indigo’s websites, as well as Barnes and Noble’s website in the U.S. […] We’re receiving Yaga orders from all over the world,” she said, adding that approximately 80 per cent of the surprise sales have been e-books. “Iceland, Hungary, Australia, Poland, Romania, Singapore. Hong Kong. Finland. It just goes on and on. 2026-02-20 19:22:47
Gold Derby: “The fans have become very protective of these boys, and so I wanted to pass along the message that they're very grateful to you because it feels like you are still there for them even though you guys aren't filming right now.” Christina Chang: “They have a lovely friendship that should remain private to them. And yes, they are in the world thanks to playing Shane and Ilya, but I think Huddy and Connor are also able to preserve that. They're doing a really good job of handling all of this. They know I love them.” 🔗 https://www.goldderby.com/tv/2026/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-coming-out-scene-hudson-williams-connor-storrie/

original tweet

2026-02-20 19:22:47
Gold Derby: “The fans have become very protective of these boys, and so I wanted to pass along the message that they're very grateful to you because it feels like you are still there for them even though you guys aren't filming right now.” Christina Chang: “They have a lovely friendship that should remain private to them. And yes, they are in the world thanks to playing Shane and Ilya, but I think Huddy and Connor are also able to preserve that. They're doing a really good job of handling all of this. They know I love them.” 🔗 https://www.goldderby.com/tv/2026/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-coming-out-scene-hudson-williams-connor-storrie/

original tweet

2026-02-20 19:22:47
Gold Derby: “The fans have become very protective of these boys, and so I wanted to pass along the message that they're very grateful to you because it feels like you are still there for them even though you guys aren't filming right now.” Christina Chang: “They have a lovely friendship that should remain private to them. And yes, they are in the world thanks to playing Shane and Ilya, but I think Huddy and Connor are also able to preserve that. They're doing a really good job of handling all of this. They know I love them.” 🔗 https://www.goldderby.com/tv/2026/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-coming-out-scene-hudson-williams-connor-storrie/

original tweet

2026-02-20 19:17:57
Christina Chang talks about her relationship with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie to @GoldDerby “They are collectively really authentically kind people, and they're very cool, and we get along very well. They're both funny in different ways. Their humor is similar, yet different, of course, and unique to who they are. I have spent a lot of time laughing with them. We make up stupid sounds and words and laugh about it, and repeatedly say that to one another throughout the day, to the point where Jacob would be like, ‘Oh my God, if I have do this one more time, I'm going to throttle you.’” “They're just authentically creative. I don't want to use the word, but they're earnest, but not in a green way. Not, like, ‘Oh, we're new to life’, but they're just authentically interested in other people. And I think Connor is more of a teddy bear in a way. I think that's kind of the way Hudson would describe him as well. Hudson's very sweet too, but he's maybe not as ... they're both so sweet. Yeah, they're my boys.” 🔗 https://www.goldderby.com/tv/2026/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-coming-out-scene-hudson-williams-connor-storrie/

original tweet

2026-02-20 19:17:57
Christina Chang talks about her relationship with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie to @GoldDerby “They are collectively really authentically kind people, and they're very cool, and we get along very well. They're both funny in different ways. Their humor is similar, yet different, of course, and unique to who they are. I have spent a lot of time laughing with them. We make up stupid sounds and words and laugh about it, and repeatedly say that to one another throughout the day, to the point where Jacob would be like, ‘Oh my God, if I have do this one more time, I'm going to throttle you.’” “They're just authentically creative. I don't want to use the word, but they're earnest, but not in a green way. Not, like, ‘Oh, we're new to life’, but they're just authentically interested in other people. And I think Connor is more of a teddy bear in a way. I think that's kind of the way Hudson would describe him as well. Hudson's very sweet too, but he's maybe not as ... they're both so sweet. Yeah, they're my boys.” 🔗 https://www.goldderby.com/tv/2026/heated-rivalry-christina-chang-coming-out-scene-hudson-williams-connor-storrie/

Christina Chang talks about her relationship with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie to @GoldDerby “They are collectively really authentically kind people, and they're very cool, and we get along very well. They're both funny in different ways. Their humor is similar, yet different, of course, and unique to who they are. I have spent a lot of time laughing with them. We make up stupid sounds and words and laugh about it, and repeatedly say that to one another throughout the day, to the point where Jacob would be like, ‘Oh my God, if I have do this one more time, I'm going to throttle you.’” “They're just authentically creative. I don't want to use the word, but they're earnest, but not in a green way. Not, like, ‘Oh, we're new to life’, but they're just authentically interested in other people. And I think Connor is more of a teddy bear in a way. I think that's kind of the way Hudson would describe him as well. Hudson's very sweet too, but he's maybe not as ... they're both so sweet. 2026-02-20 14:32:46
With Hudson Williams at the forefront of their fashion show, Dsquared2 generated over $4 Million in EMV during Milan Men’s Fashion Week AW26. They also showcased one of the highest engagement rates compared to other brands, at 3.1%. Earned Media Value is a metric that assigns a monetary value to organic exposure, such as social media engagement. It calculates what it would have cost to achieve the same reach through paid advertising. 🔗 https://lefty.io/blog/mens-fashion-week-aw26

original tweet

2026-02-20 13:20:31
.@htTweets award “Heated Rivalry” 5/5 stars, saying that it “might be the most important queer show in years”, in new review “[Connor Storrie’s acting] is matched incredibly by Hudson Williams, whose nuanced and internal performance as Shane Hollander is all about the slightest of micro-expressions that wash over his face in the many close-ups Tierney gifts him. Watch his eyes when he sees Ilya win the cup; a moment of dichotomy between the love he feels for him and the envy of it all. What can he do about it? What does he want to choose? Together, they share a chemistry so intense and enveloping that it burns the screen.” “Like the best of shows, it urges and helps the viewer to see the world anew. When a show like ‘Heated Rivalry’ releases in India, I wonder what its implications are. The legal win for decriminalising homosexuality in India aside, there is so much work to be done in terms of the stigma and the lack of awareness. That a show like ‘Heated Rivalry’ is finally here in India is a much-needed introduction to the kind of community-focused story that raises hope.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.hindustantimes.com/entertainment/web-series/heated-rivalry-review-connor-storrie-hudson-williams-jacob-tierney-queer-show-101771505026036.html

original tweet

2026-02-20 13:20:31
.@htTweets award “Heated Rivalry” 5/5 stars, saying that it “might be the most important queer show in years”, in new review “[Connor Storrie’s acting] is matched incredibly by Hudson Williams, whose nuanced and internal performance as Shane Hollander is all about the slightest of micro-expressions that wash over his face in the many close-ups Tierney gifts him. Watch his eyes when he sees Ilya win the cup; a moment of dichotomy between the love he feels for him and the envy of it all. What can he do about it? What does he want to choose? Together, they share a chemistry so intense and enveloping that it burns the screen.” “Like the best of shows, it urges and helps the viewer to see the world anew. When a show like ‘Heated Rivalry’ releases in India, I wonder what its implications are. The legal win for decriminalising homosexuality in India aside, there is so much work to be done in terms of the stigma and the lack of awareness. That a show like ‘Heated Rivalry’ is finally here in India is a much-needed introduction to the kind of community-focused story that raises hope.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.hindustantimes.com/entertainment/web-series/heated-rivalry-review-connor-storrie-hudson-williams-jacob-tierney-queer-show-101771505026036.html

2026-02-20 10:48:59
.@IndiaToday praise Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie performance in new “Heated Rivalry” review “Hudson and Connor, who play Shane and Ilya, are revelations. Not because they dominate every frame in an obvious, scene-chewing way, but because of how effortlessly they inhabit their characters. Their performances set a new bar for onscreen intimacy, one that even many heterosexual screen pairings struggle to match. There is a quiet comfort in how they navigate vulnerability. The shift in posture when they are alone versus in public, the way bravado melts into yearning.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.indiatoday.in/entertainment/ott/story/heated-rivalry-review-hudson-williams-connor-storrie-queer-romance-raises-heat-2871350-2026-02-20

original tweet

2026-02-20 10:48:59
.@IndiaToday praise Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie performance in new “Heated Rivalry” review “Hudson and Connor, who play Shane and Ilya, are revelations. Not because they dominate every frame in an obvious, scene-chewing way, but because of how effortlessly they inhabit their characters. Their performances set a new bar for onscreen intimacy, one that even many heterosexual screen pairings struggle to match. There is a quiet comfort in how they navigate vulnerability. The shift in posture when they are alone versus in public, the way bravado melts into yearning.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.indiatoday.in/entertainment/ott/story/heated-rivalry-review-hudson-williams-connor-storrie-queer-romance-raises-heat-2871350-2026-02-20

IMO: I’m so invested and captivated by this story! I need Trevor’s full backstory immediately 😭 As a fan of the thriller genre, I love how you feel a sense of unease from Hudson’s character right away. I can’t believe this was his directorial debut. I love the pacing, the colors and cinematography are beautiful, and all of the actors give really solid performances. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-19 13:31:38
Quinn founder Caroline Spiegel says that they initially pitched a sports romance story to Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie, but that the actors wanted to narrate a fantastical story instead “They represent this new generation of sex positivity and acceptance and a lack of puritanical shame. I would love to claim to be some kind of fortune teller, but we just got really lucky [booking them].” Ember & Ice is Quinn’s most popular original series, with over 3 Million plays to date 🔗 https://www.washingtonpost.com/style/2026/02/19/audio-erotica-heated-rivalry/

2026-02-18 22:27:50
Hudson’s character is named Henry Kalles in the “Yaga” play (Kalles being a primarily Scandinavian and German name) but they changed it to Henry Park for the show! Yaga is deeply rooted in Canadian talent. The play was written by Kat Sandler, a prominent award-winning Canadian playwright and director based in Toronto. The play originally premiered at the Tarragon Theatrein Toronto. While it has since been performed by international casts in places like the U.S. (Marin Theatre) and the UK (Little Lion Theatre), its origins and first performers were Canadian. All the cast and the show runner are canadians. 🇨🇦 If you wanna know more about Yaga (the book and the play) you can order the book here: 🔗 https://a.co/d/0igr19lT 🔗 https://www.barnesandnoble.com/w/yaga-kat-sandler/1137649958

original tweet

2026-02-18 22:27:50

Yaga is deeply rooted in Canadian talent. The play was written by Kat Sandler, a prominent award-winning Canadian playwright and director based in Toronto. The play originally premiered at the Tarragon Theatrein Toronto. While it has since been performed by international casts in places like the U.S. (Marin Theatre) and the UK (Little Lion Theatre), its origins and first performers were Canadian. 2026-02-18 18:32:44
“Yaga” is based on the hit play by Canadian playwright Kat Sandler, and centers on Rapp (Noah Reid), “a private investigator who arrives in a small coastal town to investigate the disappearance of a young heir to a powerful fishery, Henry Park (Hudson Williams). He finds himself at odds with an apprehensive local detective, Carson (Clark Backo), a charismatic university professor with a taste for younger men, Katherine (Carrie-Anne Moss), and a labyrinth of enigmatic suspects, secret lives, and ancient magic.” 🔗 https://variety.com/2026/tv/news/heated-rivalry-hudson-williams-crave-yaga-carrie-anne-moss-1236666434/

original tweet

2026-02-18 18:17:13
Baba Yaga is a prominent, paradoxical witch-ogress in Slavic folklore (Russia, Ukraine, Belarus) known for living in a sentient, dancing hut perched on giant chicken legs and fenced with human bones. She flies in a giant mortar, steering with a pestle, and often acts as a dangerous, child-eating antagonist or a wise, magical helper.

original tweet

2026-02-18 18:17:13
Baba Yaga is a prominent, paradoxical witch-ogress in Slavic folklore (Russia, Ukraine, Belarus) known for living in a sentient, dancing hut perched on giant chicken legs and fenced with human bones. She flies in a giant mortar, steering with a pestle, and often acts as a dangerous, child-eating antagonist or a wise, magical helper.

She flies in a giant mortar, steering with a pestle, and often acts as a dangerous, child-eating antagonist or a wise, magical helper.

Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Metal Fist Death Punch”! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/mCtOclD0UFM?si=9WEkl2NU11FEd9Q7 IMO: I love the fight scene choreography and the way this is shot. The action doesn’t feel over-the-top and Hudson plays the villain so well. I’m going to think of this every time I see a Toyota Corolla now. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-18 14:38:44
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Metal Fist Death Punch”! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/mCtOclD0UFM?si=9WEkl2NU11FEd9Q7 IMO: I love the fight scene choreography and the way this is shot. The action doesn’t feel over-the-top and Hudson plays the villain so well. I’m going to think of this every time I see a Toyota Corolla now. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-18 14:31:41
Sophie Nélisse on filming Shane Hollander’s coming out scene with Hudson Williams in episode 5 of “Heated Rivalry” “I didn’t want her to outshine him. [Rose] was really in the passenger seat, and sometimes it’s in the eye contact and the energy of being like, ‘I will be here whenever you’re ready to talk and open up.’ There’s that beautiful moment when it cuts back to his flashbacks. I remember doing ADR after we’d shot the scene once the show was about to be released, and just knives straight to my heart. It’s what really gets me in that scene. Rose is not saying anything, it’s just him living his emotion and all of his duality, everything that’s tearing him apart. And the conflict, and you feel all of that. It’s silent. Even the images are silent. It’s so powerful. We really wanted the scene to be quiet and intimate.” “On the very first take, just watching Hudson’s inner monologue, you could see the battle in his eyes and it made me tear up instantly. We both were in a place of really listening to one another in the scene. It wasn’t about what I was going to say next. It was about, how can I make him feel seen and understood and cared for?” 🔗 https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-features/sophie-nelisse-heated-rivalry-yellowjackets-ending-interview-1236495498/

original tweet

[Rose] was really in the passenger seat, and sometimes it’s in the eye contact and the energy of being like, ‘I will be here whenever you’re ready to talk and open up.’ There’s that beautiful moment when it cuts back to his flashbacks. I remember doing ADR after we’d shot the scene once the show was about to be released, and just knives straight to my heart. It’s what really gets me in that scene. Rose is not saying anything, it’s just him living his emotion and all of his duality, everything that’s tearing him apart. And the conflict, and you feel all of that. It’s silent. Even the images are silent. It’s so powerful. We really wanted the scene to be quiet and intimate.” “On the very first take, just watching Hudson’s inner monologue, you could see the battle in his eyes and it made me tear up instantly. We both were in a place of really listening to one another in the scene. It wasn’t about what I was going to say next. It was about, how can I make him feel seen and understood and cared for?” 🔗 https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/tv/tv-features/sophie-nelisse-heated-rivalry-yellowjackets-ending-interview-1236495498/

2026-02-18 06:39:06
I think way too many people feel comfortable on this app spreading lies, being racist, and hating on someone who has done nothing but treat everyone he meets with kindness and respect. We will not stay quiet in the face of the racism and coordinated hate train he’s been facing. It doesn’t feel random - it feels calculated. And it’s even more disturbing when it’s increased by people with platforms, including journalists who should know better and do better than just using his name for attention. There’s a clear pattern of double standards, selective outrage, and harmful, false narratives being pushed. Criticism is one thing - racism, harassment, and targeted misinformation are another. If we want change, we have to stop feeding it by interacting and start mass REPORTING tweets and accounts, not engaging with bait, and refusing to amplify hateful content cause we are in our own loving bubble. We can stand up for him without turning into the same thing we’re criticizing. (Defending Hudson should never mean becoming hateful ourselves towards other people in his life). If you want to help protect him and make this platform less welcoming to all this organized hate, follow @hudsonprotect. (We are not admins of this account, we are just sharing it!)

original tweet

Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “CHAD GPT”! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/_Y4VwAL0JtM?si=aGgG0I8t5cDw6HW3 IMO: I love Hudson’s robotic voice and the unsettling, empty look in his eyes. Wrapping up his email to @GQMagazine, Hudson Williams signed off with a few parting words of advice: “Be kind to yourself, be diligent, fix your shit, and stay sexy.” 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-balenciaga-spring-2026-campaign-exclusive-interview

original tweet

2026-02-17 13:53:45

original tweet

2026-02-17 13:53:45
Wrapping up his email to @GQMagazine, Hudson Williams signed off with a few parting words of advice: “Be kind to yourself, be diligent, fix your shit, and stay sexy.” 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-balenciaga-spring-2026-campaign-exclusive-interview

original tweet

2026-02-17 13:52:57
“My time and energy is my biggest luxury now and both can be taken away from me pretty quick so it’s important to guard both with a strict priority list. I’ve always been most zen with the calm arts like reading, writing, and drawing/painting. Watercolor right now [because] I’m too lazy to clean up anything else. (…) You always hear about people thinking X, Y, and Z are gonna solve their issues, and then they get it and nothing’s fixed. But truly, nothing externally gained unfucks your mental state.” - Hudson Williams 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-balenciaga-spring-2026-campaign-exclusive-interview

original tweet

2026-02-17 13:52:57

2026-02-17 13:49:53
Hudson Williams on what he’s looking for when choosing new roles (via @GQMagazine) “It starts with the script, and comes down to the director/creator. No actor can turn shit writing into something great. And at the end of the day, a subpar director can butcher a good performance — and even a good script if they’re really bad — by fumbling the movie at so many points along the way.”

original tweet

2026-02-17 13:49:53

2026-02-17 13:29:53
Hudson Williams features in Balenciaga’s first Brand ambassadors and Friends of the House campaign entitled ‘Heart and Body’ “I chose individuals, not characters. Human beings with distinct stories, faces marked by experience, vulnerability, and nuance. I was not looking for constructed personas, but for real presence. At the center of Summer 26 is the creation of a new community — different, yet connected by shared values: respect, sensitivity, strength, freedom. Not sameness, but resonance.” - Pierpaolo Piccioli

original tweet

2026-02-17 03:29:31
Broadway actors Darren Criss and Helen J. Shen from ‘Maybe Happy Ending’ on meeting Hudson Williams (via ilyasbush) Darren: “I was so happy to meet him. I was so thrilled for his whole thing. We have a lot in common.” Helen: “They didn’t tell me he was coming. I’m sick! I walked into the pink room and there he was! The cottage! It was an ambush!”

2026-02-17 03:20:49
Hudson Williams on working with Almiro Acting Studios and what they had to say about him in return 🥹 (via almirostudios) “From the moment Hudson stepped into my class - just starting his acting journey - he’s been incredibly committed, focused, and passionate about the craft. Trusting the guidance I offered and giving his all to every assignment, he proved time and again that he deserves to be at the top. Watching him win the hearts of millions while staying the same humble, sweet, and kind young man he’s always been makes me so proud as a teacher and mentor. What a gift he is. ♥️”

original tweet

2026-02-17 03:20:49
Hudson Williams on working with Almiro Acting Studios and what they had to say about him in return 🥹 (via almirostudios) “From the moment Hudson stepped into my class - just starting his acting journey - he’s been incredibly committed, focused, and passionate about the craft. Trusting the guidance I offered and giving his all to every assignment, he proved time and again that he deserves to be at the top. Watching him win the hearts of millions while staying the same humble, sweet, and kind young man he’s always been makes me so proud as a teacher and mentor. What a gift he is. ♥️”

original tweet

2026-02-16

2026-02-16 23:59:53
Ashley Iris Gill, the B Camera Operator for ‘Heated Rivalry’, talks about how touched she was by Hudson Williams’ performance during Shane’s coming out to his mom scene “I feel like it didn’t feel like acting. It felt so real, even to me. That I’m not even joking, I actually had to tear up behind the camera. I was watching him, even though we did a bunch of takes, every take still hit. I was like, ‘Oh my gosh. I can’t even look at this scene right now.’ It was just so heartwarming and it just made me think about when I was younger and I came out the closet. So it was just like, I feel like that scene I was the most touched and that’s when I was like, ‘Yo, Hudson? Star,’ because he was just... That performance was insane.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/B_zwSY1kib4

original tweet

2026-02-16 23:59:53
Ashley Iris Gill, the B Camera Operator for ‘Heated Rivalry’, talks about how touched she was by Hudson Williams’ performance during Shane’s coming out to his mom scene “I feel like it didn’t feel like acting. It felt so real, even to me. That I’m not even joking, I actually had to tear up behind the camera. I was watching him, even though we did a bunch of takes, every take still hit. I was like, ‘Oh my gosh. I can’t even look at this scene right now.’ It was just so heartwarming and it just made me think about when I was younger and I came out the closet. So it was just like, I feel like that scene I was the most touched and that’s when I was like, ‘Yo, Hudson? Star,’ because he was just... That performance was insane.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/B_zwSY1kib4

It felt so real, even to me. That I’m not even joking, I actually had to tear up behind the camera. I was watching him, even though we did a bunch of takes, every take still hit. 2026-02-16 15:25:52
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “DOGGING”! In addition to acting in this, Hudson also co-directed and co-wrote it! Trigger warning: Themes of domestic abuse. 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/YIdyHPOqr58?si=YwFakAAh8UqcWnK2 IMO: Something I’ve noticed about Hudson’s filmography is how these films often begin with a light, comedic tone and gradually evolve into something far more profound, driven by powerful performances and meaningful messages. The acting in this is fantastic and I love the moment when Hudson breaks out of the dog character. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-15 15:13:48
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF film is “Inside Augustine Mendoza’s Quotidian Apartment”! This short film was written and directed by Hudson Williams. Revealing the dark, depressive, enraged, and unrestrained side of an artist named Augustine Mendoza as he confronts his trauma, suicidal thoughts, and internalized homophobia. 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/orZxuEFA6j8?si=XWxigjJRX4aZlRga My favorite quotes: “I don’t know how to play any instrument. But I keep [my guitar] here as a reminder to the little boy that was too ungifted to know how play any instrument, too unathletic to play ball with the other boys in the school yard, too gay to kiss the girls, too fucking much of a pussy, PUSSY, to kiss the boys…[…]” “I wonder if you stare at an abyss long enough, do you see a light?” My favourite scene is the shower scene, I can feel him being consumed by rage and I love the shot. HUDSON WILLIAMS THE WRITER YOU ARE 🤌🏻 Share your favourite scene/quote and your insights about it below!

original tweet

But I keep [my guitar] here as a reminder to the little boy that was too ungifted to know how play any instrument, too unathletic to play ball with the other boys in the school yard, too gay to kiss the girls, too fucking much of a pussy, PUSSY, to kiss the boys…[…]” “I wonder if you stare at an abyss long enough, do you see a light?” My favourite scene is the shower scene, I can feel him being consumed by rage and I love the shot. HUDSON WILLIAMS THE WRITER YOU ARE 🤌🏻 Share your favourite scene/quote and your insights about it below!

🇬🇧Hudson’s british fans organized a fundraising for “Mermaids” - an UK charity working to support transgender, non-binary and gender diverse children, young people and their families. Mermaids' vision is a safe, inclusive society where trans children and young people are empowered to live their best lives. via IlyasCigarette You can still donate here: https://www.justgiving.com/page/hudsonwilliams?utm_medium=FR&utm_source=CL

2026-02-15 09:56:01
🇮🇹 Italian fans along with @HudsonWItalia, @ConnorStorrieIT and @hritaliansource did a fundraising for two associations: one that supports people diagnosed with Attention Deficit HyperactivityDisorder (ADHD) and their families, promoting scientific dissemination and improving the quality of life of those involved and the other is Italy's leading LGBTI+ association, which since 1985 has fought for equal rights, self-determination, the overcoming of stereotypes and prejudices, and against all forms of discrimination. You can still donate: https://www.gofundme.com/f/uniti-per-lamore-connohudson-birthday-project

2026-02-15 05:10:32
🇮🇩 Indonesian fans created a fundraising campaign for Perwakos (Surabaya City Transgender Association) - a platform and shelter for elderly trans women and those in crisis. Perwakos is currently in urgent need of support, as they are struggling to cover their rent and are at risk of losing their shelter. With a shared spirit of compassion and solidarity, we aim to help them sustain this vital safe space and ensure it can continue to protect and uplift the community. You can still help here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf2x8l5EP1HQ0qFX6RfMom6EpyylzGMFV2rXtjeHe65BoXLlA/viewform via barnt.

original tweet

2026-02-15 05:09:29
.@hudconPH, @ConnorStorriePH, and @hudsonwilliamph sold fan-made keyrings, raising ₱11,650, which was then donated to The Golden Gays Manila in the names of Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie by their Filipino fans. The Golden Gays Manila is a nonprofit organization based in the Philippines composed of elderly gay men. Founded in 1975 by LGBTQ+ activist Justo Justo, the organization was established to provide essential support and assistance to aging members of the gay community. Its mission includes offering shelter, food, medical care, and emotional support to individuals who have often experienced social exclusion, family rejection, or financial instability. In addition to addressing basic needs, the organization fosters a sense of dignity, belonging, and community among its members.

original tweet

The Golden Gays Manila is a nonprofit organization based in the Philippines composed of elderly gay men. Founded in 1975 by LGBTQ+ activist Justo Justo, the organization was established to provide essential support and assistance to aging members of the gay community. Its mission includes offering shelter, food, medical care, and emotional support to individuals who have often experienced social exclusion, family rejection, or financial instability. In addition to addressing basic needs, the organization fosters a sense of dignity, belonging, and community among its members.

2026-02-15 05:04:56
. @dailyhudcon created a fundraising for The Trevor Project that is a suicide prevention and crisis intervention organization for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender, queer & questioning (LGBTQ+) young people. The Trevor Project provides free, accessible, 24/7 crisis counseling services via phone (TrevorLifeline), instant messenger (TrevorChat), and text (TrevorText) to young LGBTQ+ people in crisis. You can still donate: https://give.thetrevorproject.org/fundraiser/6957157

2026-02-15 05:02:20
🇧🇷 Brazilian update accounts (@HeatedRivalryBR, @Hudsonwbrasil, and @cstorriebr) organized a fundraiser for the first shelter in Brazil’s Northern Region dedicated to LGBT+ individuals, supporting both Brazilians and refugees who have been expelled from their homes and/or are in situations of social vulnerability. The shelter also provides social and legal guidance, as well as distributing basic food baskets and personal hygiene and cleaning kits. They raised over R$2K. You can still donate! https://apoia.se/aniversariohudcon

original tweet

🇹🇷 Hudson’s Turk fans donated 10 saplings to a memorial forest, a place where trees are planted in honor of someone or to celebrate a special occasion. 2026-02-15 04:57:49
BCC Fundraiser - Gigi (gigi_champagne on ig) contacted the BCC to set it up on their website and the fans raised over 40k in less than a month! There’s truly no better way to celebrate him than by giving back to the hospital that saved his life - a place that holds deep meaning for him and his family. Hudson has also supported the hospital through his own donations in the past, making this gesture even more special. https://fundraise.bcchf.ca/fundraisers/HudsonWilliams/hudson--s-birthday-fundraiser--

2026-02-14 15:20:19
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short films are “SUPER SUPPORT” and “SUPER SUPPORT 2”! 🐆 In addition to acting in these, Hudson also co-wrote both shorts and co-directed “SUPER SUPPORT 2” Trigger warning: Themes of suicide & suicidal ideation. 🎥 Links to watch: https://youtu.be/qMO51HA1laY?si=bEjVNMapVq4RyQiY https://youtu.be/vCgAWX8IP10?si=ljPUl-gFcp0jIoF6 IMO: Not all heroes wear capes, some of them wear tiny shorts. Some of my favorite short films of Hudson’s! The concept is creative and a little bit absurd. Hudson is also so committed to his role as Land Leopard. I’m obsessed with the scene where he’s introducing himself and falls over a table. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-14 15:20:19
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short films are “SUPER SUPPORT” and “SUPER SUPPORT 2”! 🐆 In addition to acting in these, Hudson also co-wrote both shorts and co-directed “SUPER SUPPORT 2” Trigger warning: Themes of suicide & suicidal ideation. 🎥 Links to watch: https://youtu.be/qMO51HA1laY?si=bEjVNMapVq4RyQiY https://youtu.be/vCgAWX8IP10?si=ljPUl-gFcp0jIoF6 IMO: Not all heroes wear capes, some of them wear tiny shorts. Some of my favorite short films of Hudson’s! The concept is creative and a little bit absurd. Hudson is also so committed to his role as Land Leopard. I’m obsessed with the scene where he’s introducing himself and falls over a table. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-14 15:20:19
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short films are “SUPER SUPPORT” and “SUPER SUPPORT 2”! 🐆 In addition to acting in these, Hudson also co-wrote both shorts and co-directed “SUPER SUPPORT 2” Trigger warning: Themes of suicide & suicidal ideation. 🎥 Links to watch: https://youtu.be/qMO51HA1laY?si=bEjVNMapVq4RyQiY https://youtu.be/vCgAWX8IP10?si=ljPUl-gFcp0jIoF6 IMO: Not all heroes wear capes, some of them wear tiny shorts. Some of my favorite short films of Hudson’s! The concept is creative and a little bit absurd. Hudson is also so committed to his role as Land Leopard. I’m obsessed with the scene where he’s introducing himself and falls over a table. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

I did honey. No response until now. It is what it is. I was sooo excited for the article to come thinking it would be a lovely one being nice to him to come out on his birthday. It’s so frustrating after working so hard yesterday/today and read it having your words twisted on a very hostile article.

2026-02-13 21:16:37
I said people were accusing HIM of it and Wired wrote as they wanted to :) I mentioned the racism a couple of times. We didn’t share this article cause the construction has a LOT of misunderstandings and biased opinions we do not agree with. We had NO idea they were writing something like this to to vilify the fandom with our names on it. We thought it was just an article about Hudson and hate comments. Don’t take this article in consideration, please. It’s very disappointing. :(

Lainey Lui, Traci Melchor and Max Gao will take a look at the impact of ‘Heated Rivalry’, just 3 months after the 6 episode series premiered in late 2025 on Crave. (via @etalkCTV) “The Heated Rivalry Phenomenon” is an upcoming documentary including special features interviews with Hudson Williams, Connor Storrie, Jacob Tierney, Rachel Reid, Harrison Browne and Hanna Puley. 🔗 Premieres in about 3 hours from right now: https://youtu.be/s6MCt7w1a_g

2026-02-13 20:28:06
“‘The past few months have been crazy, but I’m so grateful that my mom is with me tonight and we can be together for New Year’s,’ Heated Rivalry’s Hudson Williams told Vogue at Gold House’s inaugural Lunar New Year Celebration. ‘I love spending time with my mom. She’s the best date.’” (via @voguemagazine) 🔗 Full article: https://www.vogue.com/slideshow/hudson-williams-yerin-ha-bowen-yang-gold-house-lunar-new-year

She’s the best date.’” (via @voguemagazine) 🔗 Full article: https://www.vogue.com/slideshow/hudson-williams-yerin-ha-bowen-yang-gold-house-lunar-new-year

2026-02-13 19:43:41
“At midnight, Williams turned 25 years old; a plate of dessert (rumored to be red bean mochi) with a single candle was brought out to him, and he closed his eyes to blow it out. When we were lamenting together on how old being 25 feels, I asked him what his birthday wish was. He turned somber and thought about it for a long moment. What he wished for, he told me, was for all of his friends and loved ones to “get everything they want and wish for in life.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 Read the full article here: https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:43:41
“At midnight, Williams turned 25 years old; a plate of dessert (rumored to be red bean mochi) with a single candle was brought out to him, and he closed his eyes to blow it out. When we were lamenting together on how old being 25 feels, I asked him what his birthday wish was. He turned somber and thought about it for a long moment. What he wished for, he told me, was for all of his friends and loved ones to “get everything they want and wish for in life.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 Read the full article here: https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:43:41
“At midnight, Williams turned 25 years old; a plate of dessert (rumored to be red bean mochi) with a single candle was brought out to him, and he closed his eyes to blow it out. When we were lamenting together on how old being 25 feels, I asked him what his birthday wish was. He turned somber and thought about it for a long moment. What he wished for, he told me, was for all of his friends and loved ones to “get everything they want and wish for in life.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 Read the full article here: https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:37:55
“Although there was a black rope separating the perimeter of the room as the VIP section, Williams hovered on the edge of it for the entire night, ducking under and back over and over again. He passed around light-up Hennessy batons and head-banged when Heated Rivalry anthem “All the Things She Said” came on, and pulled strangers into his dance circle, which also included groomer Aika Flores, Timid Mag founder Henry Wu, and his mother, who he brought with him to the party (and, at some point in the night, smoked a cigar with from the corner of the room, lit by a long stem Bic lighter that I can only assume he was carrying in his crossbody bag all evening).” via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:37:55
“Although there was a black rope separating the perimeter of the room as the VIP section, Williams hovered on the edge of it for the entire night, ducking under and back over and over again. He passed around light-up Hennessy batons and head-banged when Heated Rivalry anthem “All the Things She Said” came on, and pulled strangers into his dance circle, which also included groomer Aika Flores, Timid Mag founder Henry Wu, and his mother, who he brought with him to the party (and, at some point in the night, smoked a cigar with from the corner of the room, lit by a long stem Bic lighter that I can only assume he was carrying in his crossbody bag all evening).” via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:37:55
“Although there was a black rope separating the perimeter of the room as the VIP section, Williams hovered on the edge of it for the entire night, ducking under and back over and over again. He passed around light-up Hennessy batons and head-banged when Heated Rivalry anthem “All the Things She Said” came on, and pulled strangers into his dance circle, which also included groomer Aika Flores, Timid Mag founder Henry Wu, and his mother, who he brought with him to the party (and, at some point in the night, smoked a cigar with from the corner of the room, lit by a long stem Bic lighter that I can only assume he was carrying in his crossbody bag all evening).” via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:34:24
“The DJ was playing bangers all night, but it wasn’t until Williams went down to the dance floor that people actually started to dance, and stayed dancing for hours and hours—an incredibly rare feat to pull off at a New York Fashion Week party, where, in my experience, you get a few lightly shimmying shoulders and nonchalant head nods at best ... Williams, as a first-time NYFW attendee and host, was everywhere at once—everyone who wanted to be near him got to be near him. First, he was beside the bar, exchanging a hug with Bowen Yang, and then he was on the other side of the dance floor, dropping it low to “My Neck, My Back,” and then conjuring a cowboy hat seemingly from thin air, playfully putting it on and taking it off the bopping heads of the circle that formed around him ... There is no word for his energy other than infectious, spreading out across all three floors of the venue, with Williams—who ditched his blazer and danced in a bicep-baring tank all night—at the center of it.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:34:24
“The DJ was playing bangers all night, but it wasn’t until Williams went down to the dance floor that people actually started to dance, and stayed dancing for hours and hours—an incredibly rare feat to pull off at a New York Fashion Week party, where, in my experience, you get a few lightly shimmying shoulders and nonchalant head nods at best ... Williams, as a first-time NYFW attendee and host, was everywhere at once—everyone who wanted to be near him got to be near him. First, he was beside the bar, exchanging a hug with Bowen Yang, and then he was on the other side of the dance floor, dropping it low to “My Neck, My Back,” and then conjuring a cowboy hat seemingly from thin air, playfully putting it on and taking it off the bopping heads of the circle that formed around him ... There is no word for his energy other than infectious, spreading out across all three floors of the venue, with Williams—who ditched his blazer and danced in a bicep-baring tank all night—at the center of it.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:34:24
“The DJ was playing bangers all night, but it wasn’t until Williams went down to the dance floor that people actually started to dance, and stayed dancing for hours and hours—an incredibly rare feat to pull off at a New York Fashion Week party, where, in my experience, you get a few lightly shimmying shoulders and nonchalant head nods at best ... Williams, as a first-time NYFW attendee and host, was everywhere at once—everyone who wanted to be near him got to be near him. First, he was beside the bar, exchanging a hug with Bowen Yang, and then he was on the other side of the dance floor, dropping it low to “My Neck, My Back,” and then conjuring a cowboy hat seemingly from thin air, playfully putting it on and taking it off the bopping heads of the circle that formed around him ... There is no word for his energy other than infectious, spreading out across all three floors of the venue, with Williams—who ditched his blazer and danced in a bicep-baring tank all night—at the center of it.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:26:47
“A tangible vibe shift spread across the venue when the hosts joined the party, and wherever Williams went, a bulky throng followed as if magnetized; when he ducked into the mahjong room to play with his ‘Heated Rivalry’ co-star Ksenia Daniela, a crowd hovered outside, people leaning around security to catch a glimpse of him through the curtained doorway. I need to emphasize that the guests at this party were largely industry professionals—journalists, actors, and entertainers who are used to being around celebrities, and probably wouldn’t bat an eye if the entire roster of Oscar nominees pulled up, much less endeavor to ask for a photo together. The effect Williams has on a room is unlike anything I’ve witnessed before—I wasn’t alive for Beatlemania, but I’m willing to bet that Hudsonmania is a stronger drug.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 Full article: https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

original tweet

2026-02-13 19:26:47
“A tangible vibe shift spread across the venue when the hosts joined the party, and wherever Williams went, a bulky throng followed as if magnetized; when he ducked into the mahjong room to play with his ‘Heated Rivalry’ co-star Ksenia Daniela, a crowd hovered outside, people leaning around security to catch a glimpse of him through the curtained doorway. I need to emphasize that the guests at this party were largely industry professionals—journalists, actors, and entertainers who are used to being around celebrities, and probably wouldn’t bat an eye if the entire roster of Oscar nominees pulled up, much less endeavor to ask for a photo together. The effect Williams has on a room is unlike anything I’ve witnessed before—I wasn’t alive for Beatlemania, but I’m willing to bet that Hudsonmania is a stronger drug.” (via @ultragloss for @GQMagazine) 🔗 Full article: https://www.gq.com/story/hudson-williams-threw-the-best-new-york-fashion-week-party-possibly-ever

2026-02-13 16:49:20
Aika Flores wishing Hudson Williams a happy birthday via Instagram #HudDay “BIG 25 for BIG G 🎈H, thank you for spreading your infectious bright light and kindness to everyone that crosses your path. I cannot begin to express the gratitude I have for you bringing me along this journey with nothing but welcoming arms and warmth. You’re one of a kind, friend. You deserve everything and more. Never change, never let anyone dim your light. I’ll fuck em up. Happy Birthday Big Gangster. Love, Tiny Gangster 💋”

original tweet

Aika Flores wishing Hudson Williams a happy birthday via Instagram #HudDay “BIG 25 for BIG G 🎈H, thank you for spreading your infectious bright light and kindness to everyone that crosses your path. I cannot begin to express the gratitude I have for you bringing me along this journey with nothing but welcoming arms and warmth. You’re one of a kind, friend. You deserve everything and more. Never change, never let anyone dim your light. I’ll fuck em up. Happy Birthday Big Gangster. Love, Tiny Gangster 💋”

2026-02-13 15:12:51
Happy #HudDay! 🥳 Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “AND…RELEASE!” Not only did Hudson act in this, but he also co-wrote and co-directed it! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/hadSVbEWUcc?si=burGVrwaPNh871-l IMO: This one is so silly and unhinged. Hudson has such a range and truly gives it his all. His microexpressions are perfect. I also love the way this ends. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-13 15:12:51
Happy #HudDay! 🥳 Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “AND…RELEASE!” Not only did Hudson act in this, but he also co-wrote and co-directed it! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/hadSVbEWUcc?si=burGVrwaPNh871-l IMO: This one is so silly and unhinged. Hudson has such a range and truly gives it his all. His microexpressions are perfect. I also love the way this ends. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-13 05:42:23
This tweet and the replies is exclusively an overflowing love section for Hudson specially #HudDay! Use the hashtag below to comment what you love the most about him 💗 I’ll start… #IlovethatHudsonW is not a mirrorball - he’s truly himself with everyone: funny without being mean and smart without being pretentious. He’s loved everywhere he goes because he’s so captivating and kind. He has such a beautiful soul, he’s never afraid to be himself, and his priority is always his loved ones. He’s so aware of so many different issues in the world that don’t even affect him personally. He has so many female friends, and he’s still so down-to-earth even after all this fame. Happy birthday, Hudson 🥺 we love and respect you deeply and forever. Your HWU 🌷🐰

2026-02-13 03:33:54
“It scared her because she thought her being an Asian lady, that it would, kind of, limit my possibilities for roles I could get. She hasn’t seen people like her on the screen [in Canada]. When I booked ‘Heated Rivalry’ due to the fact that there was a prerequisite that I’m half Asian, I think it just kind of made her see that things are changing and that there are new possibilities. So that really felt amazing.” Proud Mama 🌷🥹

original tweet

2026-02-13 03:33:54
“It scared her because she thought her being an Asian lady, that it would, kind of, limit my possibilities for roles I could get. She hasn’t seen people like her on the screen [in Canada]. When I booked ‘Heated Rivalry’ due to the fact that there was a prerequisite that I’m half Asian, I think it just kind of made her see that things are changing and that there are new possibilities. So that really felt amazing.” Proud Mama 🌷🥹

original tweet

2026-02-13 03:33:54
“It scared her because she thought her being an Asian lady, that it would, kind of, limit my possibilities for roles I could get. She hasn’t seen people like her on the screen [in Canada]. When I booked ‘Heated Rivalry’ due to the fact that there was a prerequisite that I’m half Asian, I think it just kind of made her see that things are changing and that there are new possibilities. 2026-02-12 14:56:42
Rachel Reid on her relationship with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie (via ‘Open Book With Jenna’ podcast) “It was after filming, we started chatting in a group chat, and individually, over the summer. The three of us were in a really unique position compared to everybody else because it was like, definitely our lives were probably about to change the most. (…) It was like, yeah, the three of us were about to level up in a big way, I don't think any of us thought it was going to level up the way it did. And it's been, you know, we've all been very busy now. But I think it's still nice to check in with them and just remember that in the middle of all of this, we're the same three people. Everything feels very heightened and surreal right now. But it's still very real people in it.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/open-book-with-jenna/id1707218732?i=1000749409606

original tweet

2026-02-12 14:56:42
Rachel Reid on her relationship with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie (via ‘Open Book With Jenna’ podcast) “It was after filming, we started chatting in a group chat, and individually, over the summer. The three of us were in a really unique position compared to everybody else because it was like, definitely our lives were probably about to change the most. (…) It was like, yeah, the three of us were about to level up in a big way, I don't think any of us thought it was going to level up the way it did. And it's been, you know, we've all been very busy now. But I think it's still nice to check in with them and just remember that in the middle of all of this, we're the same three people. Everything feels very heightened and surreal right now. But it's still very real people in it.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/open-book-with-jenna/id1707218732?i=1000749409606

(…) It was like, yeah, the three of us were about to level up in a big way, I don't think any of us thought it was going to level up the way it did. original tweet

2026-02-12 13:32:24
Rachel Reid on how she would describe Shane Hollander’s love language (via ‘Open Book with Jenna’ podcast) “Shane’s thing is, like, it’s very important to him that his loved ones are safe and protected. He really values privacy and having that, like, the cottage is very important to him for a reason, and his time with his family. And now Ilya has [been] brought into that, like, [he’s] part of that circle. So, I think his love language is sharing his private life with a very select few people making sure that they feel loved and protected in that space.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/open-book-with-jenna/id1707218732?i=1000749409606&r=1872

original tweet

2026-02-12 13:32:24
Rachel Reid on how she would describe Shane Hollander’s love language (via ‘Open Book with Jenna’ podcast) “Shane’s thing is, like, it’s very important to him that his loved ones are safe and protected. He really values privacy and having that, like, the cottage is very important to him for a reason, and his time with his family. And now Ilya has [been] brought into that, like, [he’s] part of that circle. So, I think his love language is sharing his private life with a very select few people making sure that they feel loved and protected in that space.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/open-book-with-jenna/id1707218732?i=1000749409606&r=1872

2026-02-12 13:32:24
Rachel Reid on how she would describe Shane Hollander’s love language (via ‘Open Book with Jenna’ podcast) “Shane’s thing is, like, it’s very important to him that his loved ones are safe and protected. He really values privacy and having that, like, the cottage is very important to him for a reason, and his time with his family. And now Ilya has [been] brought into that, like, [he’s] part of that circle. So, I think his love language is sharing his private life with a very select few people making sure that they feel loved and protected in that space.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/open-book-with-jenna/id1707218732?i=1000749409606&r=1872

original tweet

2026-02-12 13:27:10
Rachel Reid says that ‘Unrivaled’ may not be the last Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov book in her ‘Game Changer’ series (via ‘Open Book With Jenna’ podcast) “I don’t want to say last. You know, I thought ‘The Long Game’ was the last book, so you never know. I try to write these books as if they could be the last book. So I do try to wrap things up — but you know, you never know. I have in my head an idea of where they are, like, say, 10 years later. I’m not saying I’m going to get to that in this book, but I have my own head-canon.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/open-book-with-jenna/id1707218732?i=1000749409606

original tweet

2026-02-12 13:27:10
Rachel Reid says that ‘Unrivaled’ may not be the last Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov book in her ‘Game Changer’ series (via ‘Open Book With Jenna’ podcast) “I don’t want to say last. I have in my head an idea of where they are, like, say, 10 years later. I’m not saying I’m going to get to that in this book, but I have my own head-canon.” 🔗 https://podcasts.apple.com/gb/podcast/open-book-with-jenna/id1707218732?i=1000749409606

2026-02-12 09:30:57
Kolton Stewart (Carter Vaughn) on what he learnt from working with Hudson Williams on “Heated Rivalry” (via youngentmag) “He’s just a guy that’s down for anything. He’s super free in his work, and I think that’s so important in acting because that’s the main thing is to be free enough to embody any character, be game for whatever, and have no barriers. I found that was something of him that I really admired.”

original tweet

2026-02-12 09:30:57
Kolton Stewart (Carter Vaughn) on what he learnt from working with Hudson Williams on “Heated Rivalry” (via youngentmag) “He’s just a guy that’s down for anything. He’s super free in his work, and I think that’s so important in acting because that’s the main thing is to be free enough to embody any character, be game for whatever, and have no barriers. I found that was something of him that I really admired.”

2026-02-12 02:31:15
Isabela Boscov praising Hudson Williams’ performance in Heated Rivalry. Check the full review here (you can set subtitles to English) and comment: https://youtu.be/vEL_-fLymuU?si=IA4OvS7TGYKxID8G (She is a long-time critic and brazilian reporter with extensive knowledge of cinema. She runs a successful YouTube channel (over +1M subscribers), engaging with audiences on movies and series. Her reviews are widely followed for their accuracy and unique perspective, particularly in analyzing both mainstream and niche cinema. Highly reliable critic, her approval is highly regarded.)

She runs a successful YouTube channel (over +1M subscribers), engaging with audiences on movies and series. Her reviews are widely followed for their accuracy and unique perspective, particularly in analyzing both mainstream and niche cinema. Highly reliable critic, her approval is highly regarded.)

2026-02-11 15:12:23
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Buttons”! In addition to staring in it, Hudson also wrote and directed it! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/c_xiOHWYcR0?si=3cqmCA7qBaiqSCSJ IMO: This is one of my favorite short films of Hudson’s! It’s hilarious and genuinely fun. He’s able to capture the internal struggle of coming face-to-face with your repressed self through humor. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-10 21:36:16
Sophie Nélisse on filming Shane Hollander’s coming out scene in “Heated Rivalry” (via Never Offside with Julie & Cat podcast) “It was a really important scene. We wanted that scene to feel really honest and real. (…) Hudson was so generous in his performance, and just watching his inner struggle, and watching him tear up, and feel how conflicted he was, made me almost tear up, naturally. And there was just this great sense of- we both really listened to each other. There was something that felt effortless shooting that scene.” 🔗 Full interview: https://open.spotify.com/episode/5P9oHQftQyU5yJUaJyrnlh?si=cd6dbf910d3a46f7

original tweet

2026-02-10 21:36:16
Sophie Nélisse on filming Shane Hollander’s coming out scene in “Heated Rivalry” (via Never Offside with Julie & Cat podcast) “It was a really important scene. We wanted that scene to feel really honest and real. (…) Hudson was so generous in his performance, and just watching his inner struggle, and watching him tear up, and feel how conflicted he was, made me almost tear up, naturally. And there was just this great sense of- we both really listened to each other. There was something that felt effortless shooting that scene.” 🔗 Full interview: https://open.spotify.com/episode/5P9oHQftQyU5yJUaJyrnlh?si=cd6dbf910d3a46f7

original tweet

2026-02-10 21:36:16
Sophie Nélisse on filming Shane Hollander’s coming out scene in “Heated Rivalry” (via Never Offside with Julie & Cat podcast) “It was a really important scene. We wanted that scene to feel really honest and real. (…) Hudson was so generous in his performance, and just watching his inner struggle, and watching him tear up, and feel how conflicted he was, made me almost tear up, naturally. And there was just this great sense of- we both really listened to each other. There was something that felt effortless shooting that scene.” 🔗 Full interview: https://open.spotify.com/episode/5P9oHQftQyU5yJUaJyrnlh?si=cd6dbf910d3a46f7

A fan of “Heated Rivalry” called into @RTERadio1 to share their own secret love story with a fellow football player that took place 30 years ago “After two and a half years of happiness, comfortable conversations and intimacy, he disappeared from my life. I think about him every single day since. And I still love him, even though we never said it to each other.”

2026-02-10 15:15:52
IMO: Classic Lifetime movie with a super intense plot. Hudson’s role is small, but it’s still an interesting watch. It low-key reminded me of Jennette McCurdy’s I’m Glad My Mom Died (for reasons I can’t spoil), and it’s honestly scary knowing it’s based on real events. Also, both leads are Spanish speakers, and there are a few Spanish songs throughout the film that I enjoyed. We definitely need more Hudson in a varsity jacket. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies! And if you rent don’t forget to comment on Youtube!

original tweet

Jacob Tierney discussing Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie’s chemistry and relationship in recent interview with @MSignorile “One of the most amazing things to watch was this bond between them and how much they were looking out for each other. (…) We got so lucky, not only are they wonderful actors, but their relationship made it so that I could do things that I wasn’t sure I could do.” 🔗 https://youtu.be/-WufthDsKU0

2026-02-10 11:45:15
Sophie Nélisse on her bond with Hudson Williams “I’m so happy because I will be going back to shoot ‘Yellowjackets’ in Vancouver, which is where Hudson lives. So yeah, Hudson is one of the people that I’ve gotten the closest to so quickly. Our chemistry was just instant. Hudson is so warm and caring. The way he treats people around him is unlike anyone I’ve really seen. There is something that just felt so effortless and genuine about our chemistry. I think that really resonates in [‘Heated Rivalry’].” 🔗 https://www.digitaljournal.com/entertainment/sophie-nelisse-of-heated-rivalry-talks-about-starring-in-the-horror-thriller-whistle/article

original tweet

2026-02-10 11:45:15

The way he treats people around him is unlike anyone I’ve really seen. There is something that just felt so effortless and genuine about our chemistry. I think that really resonates in [‘Heated Rivalry’].” 🔗 https://www.digitaljournal.com/entertainment/sophie-nelisse-of-heated-rivalry-talks-about-starring-in-the-horror-thriller-whistle/article

2026-02-09 20:54:36
Vogue published an insightful article on male friendship and toxic masculinity. “The bromance discourse reached new heights earlier this year, when Heated Rivalry was hitting peak fame. With each interview that co-stars Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie did, it seemed they were only getting closer and closer—they even graced us with touchy-feely play-fighting, or intense eye contact. These were not just two actors hired and paid to get along: Their chemistry was palpable, even through the screen. ”We just FaceTime or call each other randomly and consistently, and that’s been my stability through this,” Williams has said of Storrie. […] for rappers like Tyler, the Creator and A$AP Rocky: Tyler even calls A$AP his “work husband.” https://www.vogue.com/slideshow/its-the-year-of-hollywood-bromances

original tweet

2026-02-09 20:54:36
Vogue published an insightful article on male friendship and toxic masculinity. “The bromance discourse reached new heights earlier this year, when Heated Rivalry was hitting peak fame. With each interview that co-stars Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie did, it seemed they were only getting closer and closer—they even graced us with touchy-feely play-fighting, or intense eye contact. These were not just two actors hired and paid to get along: Their chemistry was palpable, even through the screen. ”We just FaceTime or call each other randomly and consistently, and that’s been my stability through this,” Williams has said of Storrie. […] for rappers like Tyler, the Creator and A$AP Rocky: Tyler even calls A$AP his “work husband.” https://www.vogue.com/slideshow/its-the-year-of-hollywood-bromances

Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Brim Reaper”! Not only did Hudson act in this, but he also helped conceptualize the story! 🎥 Link to watch: https://youtu.be/Ly8ps24iS9c?si=frgFXG2r6IZjKZli IMO: I understand why Hudson says we aren’t freaky enough 😭 I can’t believe they made this in 48 hours! I love the color-grading and all three actors have amazing comedic timing. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-08 08:59:04
“Heated Rivalry” casting director Jenny Lewis on Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie (via @BBCNews) “We feel like proud mommas. There were all these stipulations - [comfortable with] sexual content, [doing] accents or other languages, skating, [being] good looking, athletic. It was a tall order and a lot of agents might have been scared off quickly, and I think in terms of Connor and Hudson, they were clearly people who were willing to go for it and embrace every part of what the show was about.” 🔗 Read more: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c3edk87vqljo

original tweet

It was a tall order and a lot of agents might have been scared off quickly, and I think in terms of Connor and Hudson, they were clearly people who were willing to go for it and embrace every part of what the show was about.” 🔗 Read more: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c3edk87vqljo

It was a tall order and a lot of agents might have been scared off quickly, and I think in terms of Connor and Hudson, they were clearly people who were willing to go for it and embrace every part of what the show was about.” 🔗 Read more: https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c3edk87vqljo
