2026-02-07 21:06:24
Hockey player body double Jonah De Simone about the dedication Hudson Williams put into learning the intricacies of hockey for ‘Heated Rivalry’ (via @TorontoStar) “De Simone tossed out a few other suggestions that stuck, including the groin stretch Hollander does in the locker room ... Williams built out the universe of his character with small details, such as scribbling the name of his mom, Yuna Hollander, on the knob of his stick. Williams showed similar dedication to learning the intricacies of the game and how to convincingly shoot the net, he added. ‘He would buzz around on the ice and like, practice his edges and stuff like that,’ De Simone said.” 🔗 Read more: https://www.thestar.com/entertainment/television/this-university-of-guelph-hockey-player-is-shane-hollanders-body-double-on-heated-rivalry/article_1bee952f-dad2-446f-86f0-8a37e2bdd1cc.html

original tweet

2026-02-07 21:06:24

Williams built out the universe of his character with small details, such as scribbling the name of his mom, Yuna Hollander, on the knob of his stick. Williams showed similar dedication to learning the intricacies of the game and how to convincingly shoot the net, he added. ‘He would buzz around on the ice and like, practice his edges and stuff like that,’ De Simone said.” 🔗 Read more: https://www.thestar.com/entertainment/television/this-university-of-guelph-hockey-player-is-shane-hollanders-body-double-on-heated-rivalry/article_1bee952f-dad2-446f-86f0-8a37e2bdd1cc.html

2026-02-07 13:38:17
Sophie Nélisse continues to speak highly of her friendship with Hudson Williams & the “Heated Rivalry” team (via @Collider) “Hudson has truly become one of my greatest friends and is someone that I feel like I can pick up the phone and call anytime. He’s one of the most caring and compassionate [people], and Connor is so soft and gentle. The chemistry that you see in the show is really what it was on set. I feel like I’m their biggest cheerleader, and I’m so happy that they’re getting the recognition that they deserve. I love that I’m able to watch the show as a fan. I remember when it started coming out because I hadn’t really seen anything, and I was watching it and sending, in the group chat, so many voice messages. I would literally gasp and scream and cry while watching it. It’s been really fun to be able to receive the show and love it like the audience has.” “I’m truly [Hudson and Connor]’s biggest fan. I adore them. I adore Jacob [Tierney], the director. This entire shoot was a love fest of just people being so passionate and so genuine. I stepped into production when they had already been shooting for two months, and there’s always that weird feeling of like, ‘I don’t really belong here. They already have all of their inside jokes and stuff.’ And they welcomed me with such open arms, and I felt like I belonged, immediately.” 🔗 https://collider.com/whistle-horror-ending-explained-sophie-nelisse-yellowjackets-final-season/

original tweet

2026-02-07 13:38:17
Sophie Nélisse continues to speak highly of her friendship with Hudson Williams & the “Heated Rivalry” team (via @Collider) “Hudson has truly become one of my greatest friends and is someone that I feel like I can pick up the phone and call anytime. He’s one of the most caring and compassionate [people], and Connor is so soft and gentle. The chemistry that you see in the show is really what it was on set. I feel like I’m their biggest cheerleader, and I’m so happy that they’re getting the recognition that they deserve. I love that I’m able to watch the show as a fan. I remember when it started coming out because I hadn’t really seen anything, and I was watching it and sending, in the group chat, so many voice messages. I would literally gasp and scream and cry while watching it. It’s been really fun to be able to receive the show and love it like the audience has.” “I’m truly [Hudson and Connor]’s biggest fan. I adore them. I adore Jacob [Tierney], the director. This entire shoot was a love fest of just people being so passionate and so genuine. I stepped into production when they had already been shooting for two months, and there’s always that weird feeling of like, ‘I don’t really belong here. They already have all of their inside jokes and stuff.’ And they welcomed me with such open arms, and I felt like I belonged, immediately.” 🔗 https://collider.com/whistle-horror-ending-explained-sophie-nelisse-yellowjackets-final-season/

He’s one of the most caring and compassionate [people], and Connor is so soft and gentle. The chemistry that you see in the show is really what it was on set. 2026-02-06 23:17:27
Casting directors Jenny Lewis and Sara Kay talk about Hudson Williams’ audition tape for ‘Heated Rivalry’ and how it stayed with them (via @CdnPressNews) “There was just something about Hudson’s tape that just sat with us. When we watched it, there was just something coming through, some depth of emotion that just sat. Even though we watched other good auditions, it was like, ‘There’s something going on there.’ And I think he’s shown that to the world for sure now.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/YS1Mk6E8_ro

original tweet

2026-02-06 23:17:27
Casting directors Jenny Lewis and Sara Kay talk about Hudson Williams’ audition tape for ‘Heated Rivalry’ and how it stayed with them (via @CdnPressNews) “There was just something about Hudson’s tape that just sat with us. When we watched it, there was just something coming through, some depth of emotion that just sat. Even though we watched other good auditions, it was like, ‘There’s something going on there.’ And I think he’s shown that to the world for sure now.” 🔗 Full interview: https://youtu.be/YS1Mk6E8_ro

original tweet

2026-02-06 21:31:34
‘Heated Rivalry’ costume designer Hanna Puley talks more about the outfits worn by Shane Hollander (via FASHION Magazine) “Notably, Shane doesn’t really think about his clothes. Instead, his loving-but-overbearing mom, Yuna—who is always looking to further his career and land new partnership deals—buys most of his clothes for him. Above all, he wants to make her proud. Puley points to one scene, where they wear similar linen shirts. ‘That was his way of showing up for his parents,’ she says.” 🔗 Read more: https://fashionmagazine.com/style/heated-rivalry-costumes/

original tweet

2026-02-06 21:31:34

Puley points to one scene, where they wear similar linen shirts. ‘That was his way of showing up for his parents,’ she says.” 🔗 Read more: https://fashionmagazine.com/style/heated-rivalry-costumes/

2026-02-06 15:44:21
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “A Girl Dies”! Link to watch (starts 00:33): https://youtu.be/l6Ss12jgbnY?si=K021-9-JG14Ita_I&t=33 IMO: This story…I support women’s rights and wrongs (for the most part). Though Hudson plays a minor, unnamed role, his performance as a party animal is fun. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-06 13:40:49
Jackson Parrell on shooting the locker room Champagne shower scene in episode 4 of “Heated Rivalry” “We didn’t actually tell Hudson we were gonna spray him with Champagne,” Just before rolling, Tierney leaned over and whispered to a few of the actors, “Start spraying him in the face.” They only had time for one take, so Williams’s reaction is “completely genuine,” Parrell says. “He didn’t know any of it was gonna happen.” 🔗 https://www.vulture.com/article/heated-rivalry-cinematographer-memes-explained.html

original tweet

2026-02-06 13:40:49
Jackson Parrell on shooting the locker room Champagne shower scene in episode 4 of “Heated Rivalry” “We didn’t actually tell Hudson we were gonna spray him with Champagne,” Just before rolling, Tierney leaned over and whispered to a few of the actors, “Start spraying him in the face.” They only had time for one take, so Williams’s reaction is “completely genuine,” Parrell says. “He didn’t know any of it was gonna happen.” 🔗 https://www.vulture.com/article/heated-rivalry-cinematographer-memes-explained.html

2026-02-06 12:56:56
Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov grace the cover of ‘d La Repubblica’ magazine in Italy to celebrate the release of “Heated Rivalry” in the country “Among the many things that were difficult to predict for the beginning of 2026, the most surprising is that a Canadian TV series dedicated to ice hockey became the cultural phenomenon of the moment. ‘Heated Rivalry’ is the most watched show ever produced in Canada, social media algorithms are flooded with fan-generated content, hockey podcasts are listened to, group watch parties are organised in bars, and the two protagonists, Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie, sit in the front row at the Milan and Paris fashion shows and also carried the Olympic torch.” Artwork by @rombutans. The magazine will be available on newsstands from tomorrow.

original tweet

2026-02-06 12:56:56
Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov grace the cover of ‘d La Repubblica’ magazine in Italy to celebrate the release of “Heated Rivalry” in the country “Among the many things that were difficult to predict for the beginning of 2026, the most surprising is that a Canadian TV series dedicated to ice hockey became the cultural phenomenon of the moment. ‘Heated Rivalry’ is the most watched show ever produced in Canada, social media algorithms are flooded with fan-generated content, hockey podcasts are listened to, group watch parties are organised in bars, and the two protagonists, Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie, sit in the front row at the Milan and Paris fashion shows and also carried the Olympic torch.” Artwork by @rombutans. The magazine will be available on newsstands from tomorrow.

2026-02-06 12:56:56
Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov grace the cover of ‘d La Repubblica’ magazine in Italy to celebrate the release of “Heated Rivalry” in the country “Among the many things that were difficult to predict for the beginning of 2026, the most surprising is that a Canadian TV series dedicated to ice hockey became the cultural phenomenon of the moment. ‘Heated Rivalry’ is the most watched show ever produced in Canada, social media algorithms are flooded with fan-generated content, hockey podcasts are listened to, group watch parties are organised in bars, and the two protagonists, Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie, sit in the front row at the Milan and Paris fashion shows and also carried the Olympic torch.” Artwork by @rombutans. The magazine will be available on newsstands from tomorrow.

original tweet

2026-02-06 12:41:14
“Heated Rivalry” casting directors Jenny Lewis and Sarah Kay share more about Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie’s first chemistry read “Even through a screen, the spark was undeniable. They were so comfortable with each other. It was a real connection. They were relaxed, joking when they weren’t in scene. There was an instant camaraderie between the two — even though they hadn’t met in person at that point.” For Hudson, the duo says finding an actor who is half-Asian was imperative, and that Williams brought a “depth of emotion” to the character’s clean-cut, reserved exterior that stood out. In their first chemistry read, Storrie and Williams captured the characters’ cat-and-mouse tension immediately. “That dynamic you see at the very beginning, where Shane can’t quite keep up with Ilya — that emotional tag they're playing was definitely there” Ultimately, it was the pair’s “generosity” towards one another that stood out. “Sometimes with group auditions, not everybody is willing to give… But [Storrie and Williams] really were there for each other and you could feel that watching. As actors, they were really giving to each other. It felt like they felt safe with each other, even in that initial stage of doing Zoom. I think that has come across in their dynamic when we watch them on social media.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.nsnews.com/the-mix/when-hudson-met-connor-inside-the-zoom-call-where-heated-rivalry-found-its-stars-11844991

original tweet

2026-02-06 12:41:14
“Heated Rivalry” casting directors Jenny Lewis and Sarah Kay share more about Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie’s first chemistry read “Even through a screen, the spark was undeniable. They were so comfortable with each other. It was a real connection. They were relaxed, joking when they weren’t in scene. There was an instant camaraderie between the two — even though they hadn’t met in person at that point.” For Hudson, the duo says finding an actor who is half-Asian was imperative, and that Williams brought a “depth of emotion” to the character’s clean-cut, reserved exterior that stood out. In their first chemistry read, Storrie and Williams captured the characters’ cat-and-mouse tension immediately. “That dynamic you see at the very beginning, where Shane can’t quite keep up with Ilya — that emotional tag they're playing was definitely there” Ultimately, it was the pair’s “generosity” towards one another that stood out. “Sometimes with group auditions, not everybody is willing to give… But [Storrie and Williams] really were there for each other and you could feel that watching. As actors, they were really giving to each other. It felt like they felt safe with each other, even in that initial stage of doing Zoom. I think that has come across in their dynamic when we watch them on social media.” 🔗 Read more here: https://www.nsnews.com/the-mix/when-hudson-met-connor-inside-the-zoom-call-where-heated-rivalry-found-its-stars-11844991

In their first chemistry read, Storrie and Williams captured the characters’ cat-and-mouse tension immediately. “That dynamic you see at the very beginning, where Shane can’t quite keep up with Ilya — that emotional tag they're playing was definitely there” Ultimately, it was the pair’s “generosity” towards one another that stood out. “Sometimes with group auditions, not everybody is willing to give… But [Storrie and Williams] really were there for each other and you could feel that watching. 2026-02-06 12:30:07
Sophie Nélisse discusses her friendship with Hudson Williams (via @iHeartRadioCA) “I have Hudson telling me, we chat a lot, and he will just text me, ‘Oh, I sat down with this director and he loved [Heated Rivalry]’, or ‘Michelle Williams called me, Gracie Abrams called me’, and I’m like ‘What do you mean?!’” 🔗 https://youtu.be/3bwbGec_ttY

Actor and director Alan Cumming talks about ‘Heated Rivalry’, stressing the need for more queer stories to be told (via elisagnyc) “There are actually less queer people and trans people on our screens than there were a year ago. There have been many shows cancelled in the last year and a half that have queer and trans characters.”

2026-02-05 21:49:31
Alexander Skarsgård and Harry Melling, stars of ‘Pillion’, discuss “Heated Rivalry” with @etalkCTV “It’s wonderful that stories like ours, that represent a community that often aren’t represented on mainstream TV or cinema, are suddenly having a platform to be heard. I’m very proud these stories exist and are landing with an audience.”

2026-02-05 14:30:39
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Off Objective”! Link to watch: https://youtu.be/nAr4MOKdqm0?si=0cjIjXjMccFGwk5o IMO: I love seeing Hudson in such a different role compared to “Heated Rivalry.” He brings a playful, charismatic energy here, and his comedic timing really stands out. I hope we see him in a full-length action movie someday. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

2026-02-05 12:14:03
According to @Variety, Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie are “expected to resurface during the [Winter Olympic] games as part of the promotional push for ‘Heated Rivalry’” Warner Bros. Discovery Southern Europe chief Alessandro Araimo refused to comment when asked if this is true Other media outlets - such as Sky Sports and Vanity Fair Italy - have also hinted at their appearance, however, it is not yet confirmed 🔗 https://variety.com/2026/tv/global/wbd-southern-europe-chief-milan-cortina-olympics-hbo-max-1236651724/

2026-02-05 10:43:42
Dylan Walsh (David Hollander) on working with Hudson Williams and the success of “Heated Rivalry” “First of all, Hudson is a real easy guy and he is so much fun to be around. There are no pretensions but he is also not a pushover. Hudson is who he is. He’s got some idiosyncrasies with how he dresses and his sense of humor, but he is a lot of fun. I just adore him. Hudson was great as Shane, and I also like who he is as a person.” “It was great to work with all of these great people! I will say that when I got there and I saw the boys, they were so committed and so good… I was hoping it would catch on. Still, I couldn’t imagine that it would become what it has become.” 🔗 https://www.digitaljournal.com/entertainment/dylan-walsh-talks-about-starring-in-heated-rivalry-series-working-with-hudson-williams-and-connor-storrie/article

original tweet

There are no pretensions but he is also not a pushover. Hudson is who he is. He’s got some idiosyncrasies with how he dresses and his sense of humor, but he is a lot of fun. I just adore him. Hudson was great as Shane, and I also like who he is as a person.” “It was great to work with all of these great people! I will say that when I got there and I saw the boys, they were so committed and so good… I was hoping it would catch on. 2026-02-04 18:18:16
Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie are the cover of World Screen Magazine (环球银幕) in China this month! (The magazine isn’t “big” in the general public sense like millions of readers but it is very well-known and highly respected within the global television and media business. Its readership includes decision-makers at networks, OTT platforms, programming departments, and content distributors across many countries and read by executives, producers, programmers, distributors and other professionals in the business worldwide) 1/3

2026-02-04 17:54:08
Masterton says he always knew Williams was going to make it big, but he didn’t expect it so soon. “Of our group of friends, he always seemed like a shining star and he was the one to watch. He just had this raw talent that you knew was going to go the whole way,” Masterton says.

2026-02-04 17:26:35
Masterton remembers the night he watched the first two episodes alongside Williams, other friends and Williams' mother. "He wanted to share that moment with us all," Masterton says. "I was feeling really awkward, like, 'Oh my god, I'm so excited to see him, but I really hope it's good because otherwise ... you're going to have to watch Hudson in the show and pretend it's good.' But we didn't have to do that because it was genuinely awesome." During that initial, intimate viewing party, everyone had noisemakers, and each time a raunchy scene came on the screen, they'd toot their horns, Masterton says. One of the friends would also lean over and cover the eyes of Williams' mother each time, too.

original tweet

2026-02-04 17:26:35
Masterton remembers the night he watched the first two episodes alongside Williams, other friends and Williams' mother. "He wanted to share that moment with us all," Masterton says. "I was feeling really awkward, like, 'Oh my god, I'm so excited to see him, but I really hope it's good because otherwise ... you're going to have to watch Hudson in the show and pretend it's good.' But we didn't have to do that because it was genuinely awesome." During that initial, intimate viewing party, everyone had noisemakers, and each time a raunchy scene came on the screen, they'd toot their horns, Masterton says. One of the friends would also lean over and cover the eyes of Williams' mother each time, too.

"I was feeling really awkward, like, 'Oh my god, I'm so excited to see him, but I really hope it's good because otherwise ... you're going to have to watch Hudson in the show and pretend it's good.' But we didn't have to do that because it was genuinely awesome." During that initial, intimate viewing party, everyone had noisemakers, and each time a raunchy scene came on the screen, they'd toot their horns, Masterton says. One of the friends would also lean over and cover the eyes of Williams' mother each time, too.

original tweet

2026-02-04 17:23:51
"He called me one day when I was at the gym and he told me that they sold to HBO. We're all just freaking," Zack Fonzovs says. "I ran around the gym like, 'F***, my boy is an HBO star." HBO picked up "Heated Rivalry" from Canadian streaming service Crave in November 2025.

original tweet

2026-02-04 17:23:51
"He called me one day when I was at the gym and he told me that they sold to HBO. We're all just freaking," Zack Fonzovs says. "I ran around the gym like, 'F***, my boy is an HBO star." HBO picked up "Heated Rivalry" from Canadian streaming service Crave in November 2025.

2026-02-04 17:23:51
"He called me one day when I was at the gym and he told me that they sold to HBO. We're all just freaking," Zack Fonzovs says. "I ran around the gym like, 'F***, my boy is an HBO star." HBO picked up "Heated Rivalry" from Canadian streaming service Crave in November 2025.

original tweet

2026-02-04 17:19:01
Langara College Film Arts' program coordinator, Jonas Quastel remembers Williams as a "fresh-faced lad with a great attitude." "He wants to do interesting stuff. He doesn't want to fall into the pretty boy situation. He wants to do cutting edge things," Quastel says. "I see him on the track like Johnny Depp, like Keanu Reeves. I see him doing edgy things and mainstream things."

original tweet

2026-02-04 17:13:27
"I said to him, 'You're going to bite off her finger and suck it like it's the blood of Christ,'" Zack Fonzovs tells USA TODAY, recalling one of the film's scenes. "It's a very profane moment, and he (Williams) just looked at me like he was ready to bite her finger right then and there. After that, I was like, 'Oh my god. He's so passionate.' I could just tell by how willing he was and how hungry he was." 🔗 https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/tv/2026/02/04/inside-hudson-williams-life-before-heated-rivalry/88401848007/

2026-02-04 16:06:51
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short films are “Over the Edge” (my favourite) & “The Interview”! Links to watch 🎥 https://youtu.be/NZqIPndhavc?si=vwaeVBYZO1ed0-GA https://youtu.be/HJfr29PmSSk?si=aATIvzHzI8S9US6c Trigger warning: themes of depression & suicidal ideation. IMO: I love how physical Hudson’s performance is in “Over the Edge,” and how relatable the discomfort is in “The Interview.” Hudson communicates so much through his micro-expressions and the way he occupies space. I love how they portray Cory as an LGBTQIA+ character in a real way, and viewing the film through that lens adds another layer to his struggle: the loneliness, the difficulty of being emotionally open, and the pressure of carrying things internally. That interpretation isn’t explicitly confirmed, but it resonates with how his pain is portrayed. Although it’s just a rumor, some fans speculate that the Cory in “Over the Edge,” is the same Cory that appears in “The Interview,” which makes it compelling to watch them side by side. Share your favourite scene and your insights about it in the replies!

original tweet

2026-02-04 15:58:15
.@reactormag on how Shane Hollander is a key step forward for Gay Asian representation whilst highlighting the rising masculinity crisis “Shane Hollander is upending western pop culture stereotypes around Asian men (…) [and] reveals the limits placed upon gay Asian men when it comes to masculinity. It’s so gratifying to see a gay Asian character as a main character in one of the biggest hits of the year.” “What Reid and Tierney understand is that the experience of an ethnic minority is similar in many ways to that of being queer. There’s a constant need to code-switch, to surveil one’s environment in order to understand which aspects of one’s identity are safe or advantageous to reveal, and a guilt in either conforming or subverting stereotypes. It’s doubly exhausting when both of these identities are at play, and when the expectations and stereotypes of both identities begin to intersect and deviate.” “‘Heated Rivalry’ succeeds because it serves as a meta-narrative about queer Asian identity itself: How much should it divulge about its sexuality versus its ethnicity? How does it conform to or subvert gender tropes? And how does its proximity to whiteness inform its success?” 🔗 Read more here: https://reactormag.com/heated-rivalry-is-a-step-forward-for-gay-asian-representation/

original tweet

2026-02-04 15:58:15
.@reactormag on how Shane Hollander is a key step forward for Gay Asian representation whilst highlighting the rising masculinity crisis “Shane Hollander is upending western pop culture stereotypes around Asian men (…) [and] reveals the limits placed upon gay Asian men when it comes to masculinity. It’s so gratifying to see a gay Asian character as a main character in one of the biggest hits of the year.” “What Reid and Tierney understand is that the experience of an ethnic minority is similar in many ways to that of being queer. There’s a constant need to code-switch, to surveil one’s environment in order to understand which aspects of one’s identity are safe or advantageous to reveal, and a guilt in either conforming or subverting stereotypes. It’s doubly exhausting when both of these identities are at play, and when the expectations and stereotypes of both identities begin to intersect and deviate.” “‘Heated Rivalry’ succeeds because it serves as a meta-narrative about queer Asian identity itself: How much should it divulge about its sexuality versus its ethnicity? How does it conform to or subvert gender tropes? And how does its proximity to whiteness inform its success?” 🔗 Read more here: https://reactormag.com/heated-rivalry-is-a-step-forward-for-gay-asian-representation/

original tweet

2026-02-04 15:58:15
.@reactormag on how Shane Hollander is a key step forward for Gay Asian representation whilst highlighting the rising masculinity crisis “Shane Hollander is upending western pop culture stereotypes around Asian men (…) [and] reveals the limits placed upon gay Asian men when it comes to masculinity. It’s so gratifying to see a gay Asian character as a main character in one of the biggest hits of the year.” “What Reid and Tierney understand is that the experience of an ethnic minority is similar in many ways to that of being queer. There’s a constant need to code-switch, to surveil one’s environment in order to understand which aspects of one’s identity are safe or advantageous to reveal, and a guilt in either conforming or subverting stereotypes. It’s doubly exhausting when both of these identities are at play, and when the expectations and stereotypes of both identities begin to intersect and deviate.” “‘Heated Rivalry’ succeeds because it serves as a meta-narrative about queer Asian identity itself: How much should it divulge about its sexuality versus its ethnicity? How does it conform to or subvert gender tropes? And how does its proximity to whiteness inform its success?” 🔗 Read more here: https://reactormag.com/heated-rivalry-is-a-step-forward-for-gay-asian-representation/

original tweet

2026-02-03 18:23:18
.@macleans: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Is Anti-Dystopia Art “‘Heated Rivalry’ offers a cinematic universe that references our own, but casts an alternate vision of a world that’s possible — a world of pleasure, mutuality, humanness, intimacy, creativity and joy. (…) It has broken through the onslaught of pop culture visions that make dystopia’s cynical, joyless futures seem inexorable precisely because it is a piece of art that not only shows us, but allows us to feel that another world is truly possible. That lets us somatically experience a future beyond what exists now, a future we all actually want. Joy in the present makes joy in the future seem plausible. And that, too, is hope.” 🔗 https://macleans.ca/culture/heated-rivalry-is-anti-dystopia-art/

original tweet

(…) It has broken through the onslaught of pop culture visions that make dystopia’s cynical, joyless futures seem inexorable precisely because it is a piece of art that not only shows us, but allows us to feel that another world is truly possible. That lets us somatically experience a future beyond what exists now, a future we all actually want. Joy in the present makes joy in the future seem plausible. And that, too, is hope.” 🔗 https://macleans.ca/culture/heated-rivalry-is-anti-dystopia-art/

2026-02-03 15:49:53
Sophie Nélisse on working with Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie, and what she hopes for Rose and Shane’s relationship in season 2 of “Heated Rivalry” “Connor and Hudson, their chemistry is exactly like what you see on screen. Like, they’re truly the bestest of friends. They were so welcoming to everyone, to the entirety of the cast and the crew. It really felt like one big family… We all had fun shooting the show without any sort of pretension and ambition to make it blow up. I’m their biggest fan and cheerleader.” “I just want Rose to keep being an ally, and keep that safe space for (Shane). Now that he’s accepted that part of himself, it’s the beginning of something that’s so much bigger. I think having that one person that you can really feel like is your rock and someone that you confide in, that doesn’t hold any sort of judgment or prejudice, and just welcomes you with open arms and loves you unconditionally — people like that in your life are extremely rare to find. Everyone would be so lucky to have a Rose in their life, so hopefully she keeps being that person for Shane.” 🔗 https://lethbridgeherald.com/entertainment/entertainment-news/2026/02/03/sophie-nelisse-dishes-on-heated-rivalry-season-2-allyship-and-bonding-on-set/

original tweet

I’m their biggest fan and cheerleader.” “I just want Rose to keep being an ally, and keep that safe space for (Shane). Everyone would be so lucky to have a Rose in their life, so hopefully she keeps being that person for Shane.” 🔗 https://lethbridgeherald.com/entertainment/entertainment-news/2026/02/03/sophie-nelisse-dishes-on-heated-rivalry-season-2-allyship-and-bonding-on-set/

2026-02-03 14:44:52
Today’s #HudsonWilliamsFF short film is “Jump”! Hudson appears as a background performer (very quick scene), but he also worked behind the scenes as a producer, grip, and lighting! 🎥 Watch here: https://youtu.be/gZwMhHWUwZw?si=lrbsUb3JCPRJLVIV Share your favorite scene and your insights in the replies! I love psychological thrillers, and this one is so well written that you feel like you’re inside her head the entire time - it really got me. I also love the pool scene; it’s beautifully filmed and directed.

original tweet

2026-02-03 08:13:04
Supinder Wraich, who portrays Constable Sabrina Sohal on ‘Allegiance’, talks about working with Hudson Williams on his first TV credit role as Junior before ‘Heated Rivalry’ “He was really good. From the beginning I remember Manny Mahal, who had cast him in the episode and written this sort of like crazy character, and he has to do all this sort of like language that we’re like, ‘What is this kid talking about? We don’t understand what he’s saying.’ And Manny looked at him and said, ‘You know what? This guy is a star.’” via jeeyuntv on Instagram

original tweet

2026-02-03 08:13:04
Supinder Wraich, who portrays Constable Sabrina Sohal on ‘Allegiance’, talks about working with Hudson Williams on his first TV credit role as Junior before ‘Heated Rivalry’ “He was really good. From the beginning I remember Manny Mahal, who had cast him in the episode and written this sort of like crazy character, and he has to do all this sort of like language that we’re like, ‘What is this kid talking about? We don’t understand what he’s saying.’ And Manny looked at him and said, ‘You know what? This guy is a star.’” via jeeyuntv on Instagram

2026-02-02

2026-02-02 22:58:50
“Hudson is not the stereotype that people expect and he deals very well with tricky situations, like a bad joke they made with carrots comparing the size of the genitals of the characters in the series. He does not make a point of pleasing those who expects him to be a 'model minority', and this causes discomfort in those who are actively racist. In all the interviews he has given so far, the artist has shown to be completely different from what the people who hate him paint him: fan of Joan Didion, voracious reader, the only person interested in talking to director Jafar Panahi ('It was just an accident') at a Hollywood party, aware of the autism that his character has and how important this is, among other things that are omitted for the sake of gratuitous hatred (and liar). And it's striking that these charges fall on him. Just like fans and k-pop artists, who need to justify themselves 10x more than Western artists who are fans of white groups, the effort needs to be quadrupled so that the result is, by leaps and bounds, similar. There is a lot of personality, dedication and even the very right to make mistakes, much more limited to non-whites, than a perfect skincare routine, which seems to be everything that this side of the globe would like to see. “Hudson Williams Effect: Rise of the 'Heated Rivalry' actor bothers” article made by Camila Monteiro to one of Brazil’s largest digital media outlets. 🔗 https://www.uol.com.br/splash/noticias/2026/02/02/efeito-hudson-williams-o-incomodo-de-um-asiatico-canadense-no-topo.htm

original tweet

2026-02-02 22:58:50
“Hudson is not the stereotype that people expect and he deals very well with tricky situations, like a bad joke they made with carrots comparing the size of the genitals of the characters in the series. He does not make a point of pleasing those who expects him to be a 'model minority', and this causes discomfort in those who are actively racist. In all the interviews he has given so far, the artist has shown to be completely different from what the people who hate him paint him: fan of Joan Didion, voracious reader, the only person interested in talking to director Jafar Panahi ('It was just an accident') at a Hollywood party, aware of the autism that his character has and how important this is, among other things that are omitted for the sake of gratuitous hatred (and liar). And it's striking that these charges fall on him. Just like fans and k-pop artists, who need to justify themselves 10x more than Western artists who are fans of white groups, the effort needs to be quadrupled so that the result is, by leaps and bounds, similar. There is a lot of personality, dedication and even the very right to make mistakes, much more limited to non-whites, than a perfect skincare routine, which seems to be everything that this side of the globe would like to see. “Hudson Williams Effect: Rise of the 'Heated Rivalry' actor bothers” article made by Camila Monteiro to one of Brazil’s largest digital media outlets. 🔗 https://www.uol.com.br/splash/noticias/2026/02/02/efeito-hudson-williams-o-incomodo-de-um-asiatico-canadense-no-topo.htm

2026-02-02 22:07:57
I feel like I should share this because I know how much you all care about Hudson, and I can see how upsetting this situation has been for many of you: Hudson’s legal team is aware of the hate tweets, group chats, and accounts created with the sole intention of defaming him. They will do their work but here’s what we should do: It’s also important to say that these cowardly accounts, runned through burner profiles do not deserve your time, energy, or attention. Right now, the most important thing is for you to take care of your mental health. The smartest thing to do is not engage, only report. I’ve seen a lot of people quoting these accounts, and that’s exactly what they want: attention. Engaging will only upset you further, and it’s a battle that can’t be won. Unfortunately, some people will hate for no reason, and there’s nothing we can do to change their minds. What we can do is focus on Hudson, his work, and on ourselves - so this space stays positive and safe for everyone. If you stop engaging, this content won’t show up on your timeline, and that’s one of the best forms of self-care you can practice right now. Hudson is thriving and skyrocketing, and no amount of online hate can take that away from him if we are here to support him and his work spreading it. Mwah and PLEASE take care of yourself.

original tweet

2026-02-02 22:07:57
I feel like I should share this because I know how much you all care about Hudson, and I can see how upsetting this situation has been for many of you: Hudson’s legal team is aware of the hate tweets, group chats, and accounts created with the sole intention of defaming him. They will do their work but here’s what we should do: It’s also important to say that these cowardly accounts, runned through burner profiles do not deserve your time, energy, or attention. Right now, the most important thing is for you to take care of your mental health. The smartest thing to do is not engage, only report. I’ve seen a lot of people quoting these accounts, and that’s exactly what they want: attention. Engaging will only upset you further, and it’s a battle that can’t be won. Unfortunately, some people will hate for no reason, and there’s nothing we can do to change their minds. What we can do is focus on Hudson, his work, and on ourselves - so this space stays positive and safe for everyone. If you stop engaging, this content won’t show up on your timeline, and that’s one of the best forms of self-care you can practice right now. Hudson is thriving and skyrocketing, and no amount of online hate can take that away from him if we are here to support him and his work spreading it. Mwah and PLEASE take care of yourself.

I feel like I should share this because I know how much you all care about Hudson, and I can see how upsetting this situation has been for many of you: Hudson’s legal team is aware of the hate tweets, group chats, and accounts created with the sole intention of defaming him. They will do their work but here’s what we should do: It’s also important to say that these cowardly accounts, runned through burner profiles do not deserve your time, energy, or attention. Right now, the most important thing is for you to take care of your mental health. The smartest thing to do is not engage, only report. I’ve seen a lot of people quoting these accounts, and that’s exactly what they want: attention. Engaging will only upset you further, and it’s a battle that can’t be won. Unfortunately, some people will hate for no reason, and there’s nothing we can do to change their minds. What we can do is focus on Hudson, his work, and on ourselves - so this space stays positive and safe for everyone. If you stop engaging, this content won’t show up on your timeline, and that’s one of the best forms of self-care you can practice right now. Hudson is thriving and skyrocketing, and no amount of online hate can take that away from him if we are here to support him and his work spreading it. Mwah and PLEASE take care of yourself.

2026-02-02 20:27:12
Clinical and sports psychologist Tess M. Kilwein on the impact “Heated Rivalry” is having on LGBTQ+ sports players “‘Heated Rivalry’ refuses to strip its characters of masculinity or competitiveness to make queerness palatable. [Shane & Ilya] are strong, dominant, high-performing athletes. That matters, as research has consistently shown that rigid stereotypes linking athleticism with heterosexuality remain a core barrier to inclusion in sport environments.” “It is not just about seeing queer athletes exist. It is about seeing their lived realities understood. The compromises. The isolation. The exhaustion of adapting to systems that reward performance while punishing authenticity.” 🔗 Read more here: https://lifestyle.inquirer.net/562862/is-heated-rivalry-the-thing-to-change-attitudes-about-gay-pro-athletes/

original tweet

2026-02-02 20:27:12

2026-02-02 20:17:41
.@preenph: Is ‘Heated Rivalry’ the thing to change attitudes about gay pro athletes? “The smash boys’ love series may be inspiring a new generation of gay athletes to come out, a decade after Jason Collins. It’s not just your imagination: The writing on ‘Heated Rivalry’ is powerful, accurate, and effective enough to cause these effects on athletes. The experts actually agree.” https://lifestyle.inquirer.net/562862/is-heated-rivalry-the-thing-to-change-attitudes-about-gay-pro-athletes/

original tweet

2026-02-02 19:50:28
“While physical intimacy is the hook of ‘Heated Rivalry’, it’s a different quality that has turned the show into a full-on phenomenon in the month since its finale aired. Plenty of other romances have been this sexy (here’s looking at you, Bridgerton), but few have been this nuanced with their character work. And it’s that delicate exploration of emotional desire that has people watching the series over and over again.” - @TheAVClub

original tweet

2026-02-02 19:50:28
“While physical intimacy is the hook of ‘Heated Rivalry’, it’s a different quality that has turned the show into a full-on phenomenon in the month since its finale aired. Plenty of other romances have been this sexy (here’s looking at you, Bridgerton), but few have been this nuanced with their character work. And it’s that delicate exploration of emotional desire that has people watching the series over and over again.” - @TheAVClub

2026-02-02 19:50:28
“While physical intimacy is the hook of ‘Heated Rivalry’, it’s a different quality that has turned the show into a full-on phenomenon in the month since its finale aired. Plenty of other romances have been this sexy (here’s looking at you, Bridgerton), but few have been this nuanced with their character work. And it’s that delicate exploration of emotional desire that has people watching the series over and over again.” - @TheAVClub

original tweet

2026-02-02 19:45:46
.@TheAVClub on episode 5 of “Heated Rivalry” “It’s the episode where Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie ratchet up their already strong performances to a new level. Particularly on rewatch, you can really appreciate how Williams totally shifts Shane’s level of confidence after his girlfriend Rose Landry (Sophie Nélisse) gently but firmly ushers him out of the closet and into a new level of self-understanding. For all the beloved sequences in episode five, an underrated one involves Shane coming out as gay and pushing Ilya to admit they have something real. Tierney sets it up like a scene that’s going to serve as a moment of catharsis for Shane only to unexpectedly pivot to one of catharsis for Ilya — who’s suddenly talking about Russia and his family in a way he never has in the past nine years. One act of openness begets another. It’s the moment Ilya mentally shifts Shane from the guy he texts for a hookup to the person he calls when his father dies — an opening of the emotional floodgates he can at first only acknowledge in Russian.” 🔗 https://www.avclub.com/heated-rivalry-mirrored-desires-that-make-show-so-addictive

original tweet

2026-02-02 19:45:46
.@TheAVClub on episode 5 of “Heated Rivalry” “It’s the episode where Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie ratchet up their already strong performances to a new level. Particularly on rewatch, you can really appreciate how Williams totally shifts Shane’s level of confidence after his girlfriend Rose Landry (Sophie Nélisse) gently but firmly ushers him out of the closet and into a new level of self-understanding. For all the beloved sequences in episode five, an underrated one involves Shane coming out as gay and pushing Ilya to admit they have something real. Tierney sets it up like a scene that’s going to serve as a moment of catharsis for Shane only to unexpectedly pivot to one of catharsis for Ilya — who’s suddenly talking about Russia and his family in a way he never has in the past nine years. One act of openness begets another. It’s the moment Ilya mentally shifts Shane from the guy he texts for a hookup to the person he calls when his father dies — an opening of the emotional floodgates he can at first only acknowledge in Russian.” 🔗 https://www.avclub.com/heated-rivalry-mirrored-desires-that-make-show-so-addictive

original tweet

2026-02-02 19:45:46
.@TheAVClub on episode 5 of “Heated Rivalry” “It’s the episode where Hudson Williams and Connor Storrie ratchet up their already strong performances to a new level. Particularly on rewatch, you can really appreciate how Williams totally shifts Shane’s level of confidence after his girlfriend Rose Landry (Sophie Nélisse) gently but firmly ushers him out of the closet and into a new level of self-understanding. For all the beloved sequences in episode five, an underrated one involves Shane coming out as gay and pushing Ilya to admit they have something real. Tierney sets it up like a scene that’s going to serve as a moment of catharsis for Shane only to unexpectedly pivot to one of catharsis for Ilya — who’s suddenly talking about Russia and his family in a way he never has in the past nine years. One act of openness begets another. It’s the moment Ilya mentally shifts Shane from the guy he texts for a hookup to the person he calls when his father dies — an opening of the emotional floodgates he can at first only acknowledge in Russian.” 🔗 https://www.avclub.com/heated-rivalry-mirrored-desires-that-make-show-so-addictive

original tweet

2026-02-02 19:34:43
.@TheAVClub praise Hudson Williams’ acting, the character work, and more in new “Heated Rivalry” write-up “Indeed, [the show] is filled with the kind of poignantly written, brilliantly acted scene work that so often becomes the breakout moments of prestige fantasy dramas or superhero movies or sci-fi shows. But what’s so special about the romance genre is that it doesn’t have to smuggle its character arcs into some kind of larger plot-driven package. The character work is the story engine in a way that’s singularly rewarding, whether on a first watch or a fifth. William Faulkner once said that the only thing worth writing about is ‘the human heart in conflict with itself.’ And few shows have explored that as purely — or as carnally — as ‘Heated Rivalry’. (...) It’s a beautifully subtextual exploration of the sort of real-life communication problems that actually keep people apart rather than the plot contrivances rom-coms so often reach for.” 🔗 Read more here (and we highly recommend that you do): https://www.avclub.com/heated-rivalry-mirrored-desires-that-make-show-so-addictive

original tweet

But what’s so special about the romance genre is that it doesn’t have to smuggle its character arcs into some kind of larger plot-driven package.
